Draft ONC 2024 SVAP Syndromic Surveillance

This reflects the draft updated test plan and IGAMT under review with CDC SS SMEs

Test Objectives

The Following test objectives are satisfied in this test plan:

This test plan includes examining a Health IT Module's ability to create an ADT^A04-Registration message, an ADT^A08-Update message, an ADT^A03-Discharge message, and an ADT^A01-Admission message within HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019, conformance requirements.

  • Setting (IP, ED, Urgent Care, Ambulatory)
  • Chief complaint is captured as a coded value in PV2-3 Admit Reason
  • Chief complaint in free-text
  • Diagnoses are submitted test using SNOMED-CT codes:Per ASTP/ONC, for certification testing HIT developers must demonstrate that their system supports the ICD-10 CM and SNOMED CT value set (this policy is conformant to the named standard HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019). ICD-10 CM codes are provided/required in the Test Data for coding the diagnoses in this Test Case
  • Diagnoses are submitted test using ICD-10-CM codes:Per ASTP/ONC, for certification testing HIT developers must demonstrate that their system supports the ICD-10 CM and SNOMED CT value set (this policy is conformant to the named standard HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019). ICD-10 CM codes are provided/required in the Test Data for coding the diagnoses in this Test Case
  • Patient Name is Identifiable n the submissions: Per ASTP/ONC, for certification testing HIT developers must demonstrate that their system supports the Patient Name and pseudonymized data in PID-5 (this policy is conformant to the named standard HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019).The patient name presented in a pseudonymized manner is required for this Test Step.
  • Patient name is pseudonymized in the submissions: Per ASTP/ONC, for certification testing HIT developers must demonstrate that their system supports the Patient Name and pseudonymized data in PID-5 (this policy is conformant to the named standard HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019).The patient name presented in a pseudonymized manner is required for this Test Step.
  • Multiple races
  • Patient's demographic information is unavailable at registration
  • Updates to Diagnoses
  • Discharge Disposition Home
  • Discharge Disposition Expired
  • Update Diagnosis Post-Discharge
  • Facility sending the syndromic surveillance message is different than the facility where the patient received care
  • Sending required observations where known or applicable 
    • pregnancy, 
    • travel
    • Tobacco use
    • Initial acuity
    • Body Height
    • Body Weight
    • BMI
    • Triage notes
    • Patient Location
    • Chief Complaint
    •  Treating Facility Location
    • Age
      • Pediatric case where age is measured in months.
      • Adult case where age is measured in years.

  • Timeliness
  • Consistency across messages

Evaluation Criteria
null

Notes

 Validation Exceptions:

1. HCPT is an acceptable code (in addition to HCPTNUCC) for OBX-5.3 for the SS003 co-constraint. See the NIST-7 clarification.

2. I10 is an acceptable code (in addition to I10C) for DG1-3.3. See NIST-6 clarification.


1. Urgent Care Visit

Patient is seen in Urgent Care Center and is discharged/dispositioned to home.

Test Story

Description
null

Comments

Pre-condition

Post-Condition

Test Objectives
null

Evaluation Criteria
null

Notes
null

1.1. SS-UC-1_UC_Visit_Influenza_Child

Test case addresses a child brought to an urgent care center with influenza and stenosis of external ear canal due to inflammation.

Test Story

Description

Syndromic surveillance is public health surveillance that emphasizes the use of near "real-time" health data and statistical tools for disease or hazardous event detection, situation awareness for mass gatherings and public health emergencies, and ad hoc and population health trend analyses. For syndromic surveillance purposes, required data on all clinical encounters must be sent to the jurisdictional public health agency within 12 hours of any given encounter, and in accordance with local or state laws and practices.
The Influenza Child Test Case provides an example of a clinical encounter that could take place in an urgent care setting.. This test case also provides an example where the facility sending the syndromic surveillance message is different than the facility where the patient received care. In this test case, an infant is seen at an urgent care center for fever, cough, and earache. Diagnostic tests show that he has influenza and stenosis of the external ear canal. He is discharged to home with treatment instructions and a prescription. Syndromic surveillance data about the visit are transmitted to the local health department.


This test case involves 3 steps:

  • a registration message (ADT^A04),
  • followed by an update message (ADT^A08),
  • followed by a discharge message (ADT^A03).


Messages in this test case will be used in a Manual Test Step under functional requirement testing for validating the HIT capability of populating Visit ID (PV1-19.1) and Visit Time (PV1-44.1) each with the same value for all messages included in this Test Case.



Comments

No Comments


Pre-condition

No Pre-Condition


Post-Condition

No Post-Condition


Test Objectives

This test case examines a Health IT Module's ability to create an ADT^A04-Registration message, an ADT^A08-Update message, and an ADT^A03-Discharge message within HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019, conformance

The following test objectives and variants are supported by this test case:

  • Setting is Urgent Care.
  • Chief Complaint is captured as free text.
  • Patient name is de-identified in the submissions: Per ASTP/ONC, for certification testing HIT developers must demonstrate that their system supports the Patient Name and de-identified data in PID-5 (this policy is conformant to the named standard HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019).The patient name presented in a de-identified manner is required for this Test Step.
  • Diagnoses are submitted test using either ICD-10-CM or SNOMED-CT: Per ASTP/ONC, for certification testing  a Health IT Module can use either the ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT® value sets in the submitted messages for all of the test steps in the Syndromic Surveillance Test Suite. Where the tool does not have test data that supports the Health IT Module’s value set (either ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT®), the developer of the Health IT Module must provide the codes and testers must perform a visual inspection of the messages for these test steps to ensure that equivalent and valid ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT® are used to populate the messages.  This policy is conformant to the named standard HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019). ICD-10-CM codes are provided/required in the Test Data for coding the diagnoses in this Test Case.
  • Pediatric case where age is measured in months.
  • Updates to Diagnoses
  • Multiple Races
  • Insurance is known

Evaluation Criteria

No Evaluation Criteria


Notes

None


1.1.1. SS-UC-1.1_Registration_A04

Patient is registered at an Urgent Care Center and is assigned a working diagnosis.

Test Story

Description

A mother brings her 6-month old male infant to NIST Hospital`s Urgent Care on February 20, 2025, at 8:30 AM. A clerical assistant registers the patient. She records the patient`s name, date of birth, race, ethnicity, residence, insurance information (PlanID123 from MyInsuranceCompany), and health history. The clerical assistant also records the patient's chief complaint in free-text is, "Fever, cough, and earache." NIST Hospital's Urgent Care is an outpatient facility operated by NIST Hospital. 

NIST Hospital routinely sends electronic syndromic surveillance data to the City Health Department (NIST PH SS FAC 1) in accordance  with a city regulation for all of it's facilities within that municipal jurisdiction. At 10:00 AM, NIST Hospital`s electronic health record module for syndromic surveillance data assembles and transmits a Registration ADT message about this patient encounter on behalf of the Urgent Care facility as part of an automated process that transmits new or updated content every 15 minutes. The patient name and birthdate are de-identified in the submission, so no date of birth is included, and the name is represented with name type 'S' to indicate that the name is empty for anonymity.

The dates within the message and across the messages in this test case will be subject to visual inspection to verify data consistencies and the ability to conform to the requirement to send this message within 12 hours of the event triggering this message.



Comments

Dates and times are provided in this test scenario to illustrate the sequence of clinical and messaging events. Since the exact dates and times are not reproducible when modeling the test scenario with HIT, only date and time format will be validated within tester submitted test data. 


Pre-condition

No Pre-Condition


Post-Condition

No Post-Condition


Test Objectives

This test step examines an HIT Module's ability to create an ADT^A04 Registration message within HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019, conformance requirements.

The following test objectives and variants are supported by this test case:

  • Setting is Urgent Care.
  • Chief complaint is submitted as free-text.
  • Patient name is de-identified in the submissions: Per ASTP/ONC, for certification testing HIT developers must demonstrate that their system supports the Patient Name and de-identified data in PID-5 (this policy is conformant to the named standard HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019).The patient name presented in a de-identified manner is required for this Test Step.
  • Pediatric case where age is measured in months.
  • Multiple Races
  • Insurance is known

Evaluation Criteria

Message Validation Report


Notes

Although the other units of measure for patient age are acceptable in general (and the Context-free validation accepts any of the valid units of measure for age), this Scenario specifies that "mo" for months be used in this message for the Context-based validation.

Messages in this test case will be used in a Manual Test Step under functional requirement testing.

The MSH-4 value is agreed upon by the sender and receiver.

1.1.2. SS-UC-1.2_Update_A08

The clinician sees the patient and adds a working diagnosis, demographic updates, and procedure information.

Test Story

Description

At 9:00 AM a nurse sees the patient and performs a vital sign assessment, noting that the child currently has a temperature of 101.2 with a productive cough and right ear inflammation and fluid build-up.

At 9:10 AM, the physician assigns the patient the working diagnoses of influenza with other respiratory manifestations (ICD-10 CM diagnosis code of Influenza due to identified novel influenza A virus with other respiratory manifestations  J09.X2), and Acquired stenosis of external ear canal secondary to inflammation and infection, unspecified ear (ICD-10 CM diagnosis code of H61.329 ) and documents the information in the patient`s electronic medical record. At 9:15  AM the physician orders a rapid influenza test, chest x-ray, and a treatment. The patient`s vital signs gradually return to normal. A nurse weighs the patient, measures his length, and charts the data in the patient’s record. This information is used by the physician when ordering the take-home prescription.

 NIST Hospital's Urgent Care is an outpatient facility operated by NIST Hospital that routinely sends electronic syndromic surveillance data to the the City Health Department (NIST PH SS FAC 1) in accordance with a city regulation. At 9:15 AM, NIST Hospital`s electronic health record module for syndromic surveillance data assembles and transmits an Update ADT message with the working diagnoses and the height and weight information for this patient encounter on behalf of the Urgent Care facility as part of an automated process that transmits new or updated content every 15 minutes.  The patient name and birthdate are de-identified in the submission, so no date of birth is included, and the name is represented with name type 'S' to indicate that the name is empty for anonymity.


. 









Comments

Dates and times are provided in this test scenario to illustrate the sequence of clinical and messaging events. Since the exact dates and times are not reproducible when modeling the test scenario with HIT, only date and time format will be validated within tester submitted test data


Pre-condition

A04-Registration message is sent before the A08-Update message.


Post-Condition

Patient record is updated with procedure results, diagnoses and patient height/weight. An ADT*A08 message has been generated with this updated content.


Test Objectives

This test step examines a Health IT Module's ability to create ADT^A08 Update message within HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019 conformance requirements.

The following test objectives and variants are supported by this test case:

  • Setting is Urgent Care.
  • Chief complaint is submitted as free-text.
  • Patient name is de-identified in the submissions: Per ASTP/ONC, for certification testing HIT developers must demonstrate that their system supports the Patient Name and de-identified data in PID-5 (this policy is conformant to the named standard HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019).The patient name presented in a de-identified manner is required for this Test Step.
  • Diagnoses are submitted test using either ICD-10-CM or SNOMED-CT: Per ASTP/ONC, for certification testing  a Health IT Module can use either the ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT® value sets in the submitted messages for all of the test steps in the Syndromic Surveillance Test Suite. Where the tool does not have test data that supports the Health IT Module’s value set (either ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT®), the developer of the Health IT Module must provide the codes and testers must perform a visual inspection of the messages for these test steps to ensure that equivalent and valid ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT® are used to populate the messages.  This policy is conformant to the named standard HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019). ICD-10-CM codes are provided/required in the Test Data for coding the diagnoses in this Test Case.
  • Pediatric case where age is measured in months.
  • Updates to Diagnoses
  • Multiple Races
  • Insurance is known

Evaluation Criteria

Message Validation Report

Diagnosis code (DG1-3) validation is validated as follows:

The table below summarizes the format representations that are accepted for ONC Health IT Module Certification. Rows 1 and 2 show the formats as required by the implementation guide and standard terminology rules and best practices. The last 3 columns show acceptable alternative formats. For this test case one of the following values shall be present in the message:

Yes

The NIST column indicates what the NIST Tool Validation will accept in context-based testing (used for ONC Certification). A ‘Yes’ in the NIST column signifies that the tool will accept the format and not throw an error. A ‘No’ in the NIST column signifies that the tool will throw an error (specifically for DG1-3.3). However, based on the recommendation from the CDC SMEs and approval from ASTP/ONC the format is acceptable. The NIST test cases documents the instances where the test proctor can grant an exception when the tool issues and error.


Notes

ICD-10 CM codes are provided/required in the Test Data for coding the diagnoses in this Test Case. If the HIT developer uses different but equivalent ICD-10 CM codes than the ones provided, the Tester may ignore errors generated by the Test Tool related to incorrect code when the ICD-10 CM code data used in the message are determined via visual inspection to be valid data.

ICD-10 CM codes are acceptable with or without decimals.

Although the other units of measure for patient age are acceptable in general (and the Context-free validation accepts any of the valid units of measure for age), this Scenario specifies that "mo" for months be used in this message for the Context-based validation. Messages in this test case will be used in a Manual Test Step under functional requirement testing.

1.1.3. SS-UC-1.3_Discharge_A03

Patient is discharged from Urgent Care Center and is assigned a final diagnosis.

Test Story

Description

At 10 AM, the patient`s laboratory and radiology tests results are provided to the attending physician. The tests indicate that the infant has influenza and does not have pneumonia. The patient`s mother is provided with treatment information and prescriptions for her infant son. The working diagnoses (ICD-10 CM J09.X2 Influenza due to identified novel influenza A virus with other respiratory manifestations and H61.329 Acquired stenosis of external ear canal secondary to inflammation and infection, unspecified ear) are updated as final diagnoses. At 10:20 AM the patient is discharged home. 

NIST Hospital's Urgent Care is an outpatient facility operated by NIST Hospital that routinely sends electronic syndromic surveillance data to the the City Health Department (NIST PH SS FAC 1) in accordance with a city regulation. At 10:30 AM on February 20, 2025, NIST Hospital`s electronic health record module for syndromic surveillance data assembles and transmits a Discharge ADT message about this patient encounter on behalf of the Urgent Care facility as part of a routine that transmits new or updated content every 15 minutes. The patient name and birthdate are de-identified in the submission, so no date of birth is included, and the name is represented with name type 'S' to indicate that the name is empty for anonymity.


Comments

Dates and times are provided in this test scenario to illustrate the sequence of clinical and messaging events. Since the exact dates and times are not reproducible when modeling the test scenario with HIT, only date and time format will be validated within tester submitted test data. 

Automated triggering in this test case is an example for the test story, but there is no requirement that the System Under Test demonstrate support for such automated triggers.


Pre-condition

A08-Update message is sent before the A03-Disposition message.


Post-Condition

No Post-Condition


Test Objectives

This test step examines a Health IT Module's ability to create an ADT^A03 Disposition message within HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019 conformance requirements.

The following test objectives and variants are supported by this test case:

  • Setting is Urgent Care.
  • Chief complaint is submitted as free-text.
  • Patient name is de-identified in the submissions: Per ASTP/ONC, for certification testing HIT developers must demonstrate that their system supports the Patient Name and de-identified data in PID-5 (this policy is conformant to the named standard HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019).The patient name presented in a de-identified manner is required for this Test Step.
  • Diagnoses are submitted test using either ICD-10-CM or SNOMED-CT: Per ASTP/ONC, for certification testing  a Health IT Module can use either the ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT® value sets in the submitted messages for all of the test steps in the Syndromic Surveillance Test Suite. Where the tool does not have test data that supports the Health IT Module’s value set (either ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT®), the developer of the Health IT Module must provide the codes and testers must perform a visual inspection of the messages for these test steps to ensure that equivalent and valid ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT® are used to populate the messages.  This policy is conformant to the named standard HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019). ICD-10-CM codes are provided/required in the Test Data for coding the diagnoses in this Test Case.
  • Pediatric case where age is measured in months.
  • Updates to Diagnoses
  • Multiple Races
  • Insurance is known

Evaluation Criteria

The NIST column indicates what the NIST Tool Validation will accept in context-based testing (used for ONC Certification). A ‘Yes’ in the NIST column signifies that the tool will accept the format and not throw an error. A ‘No’ in the NIST column signifies that the tool will throw an error (specifically for DG1-3.3). However, based on the recommendation from the CDC SMEs and approval from ASTP/ONC the format is acceptable. The NIST test cases documents the instances where the test proctor can grant an exception when the tool issues and error.


Notes

ICD-10 CM codes are provided/required in the Test Data for coding the diagnoses in this Test Case. If the HIT developer uses different but equivalent ICD-10 CM codes than the ones provided, the Tester may ignore errors generated by the Test Tool related to incorrect code when the ICD-10 CM code data used in the message are determined via visual inspection to be valid data.

ICD-10 CM codes are acceptable with or without decimals.

Although the other units of measure for patient age are acceptable in general (and the Context-free validation accepts any of the valid units of measure for age), this Scenario specifies that "mo" for months be used in this message for the Context-based validation.

1.2. SS-UC-2_UC_Visit_Hurricane_Impact

Test case addresses an adult patient seeking care at an urgent care center with gastrointestinal symptoms after exposure hurricane-related floods.

Test Story

Description

Syndromic surveillance is public health surveillance that emphasizes the use of near "real-time" health data and statistical tools for disease or hazardous event detection, situation awareness for mass gatherings and public health emergencies, and ad hoc and population health trend analyses. For syndromic surveillance purposes, required data on all clinical encounters must be sent to the jurisdictional public health agency within 12 hours of any given encounter, and in accordance with local or state laws and practices.
The Hurricane Impact Case provides an example of a clinical encounter that could take place in a freestanding urgent care setting.

In this test case, an adult male seeks care for gastrointestinal symptoms. He had been exposed to flood waters following a recent hurricane, and is experiencing vomiting, diarrhea, nausea, and abdominal pain. He is diagnosed with dehydration and treated with IV fluids, and given an injectable antiemetic to help with vomiting. The clinician determines that further investigation and treatment are better handled in an Emergency Room setting, and refers the patient for Emergency care. The jurisdiction requires that the syndromic surveillance messages are sent in with identifiable demographics.  Syndromic surveillance data about the visit are transmitted to the state health department. This Test Case will test using SNOMED-CT Dx codes and full, legal patient name.  This test case includes documentation of travel and addition of a final diagnosis post-discharge.

This test case involves 3 steps:

  • a registration message (ADT^A04),
  • followed by an update message (ADT^A08),
  • followed by a discharge message (ADT^A03).

Messages in this test case will be used in a Manual Test Step under functional requirement testing for validating the HIT capability of populating Visit ID (PV1-19.1) and Visit Time (PV1-44.1) each with the same value for all messages included in this Test Case.






Comments

No Comments


Pre-condition

No Pre-Condition


Post-Condition

No Post-Condition


Test Objectives

This test case examines a Health IT Module's ability to create an ADT^A04-Registration message, an ADT^A08-Update message, and an ADT^A03-Discharge message within HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019, conformance requirements.

The following test objectives and variants are supported by this test case:

  • Setting is Urgent Care.
  • Chief complaint is captured as a narrative in PV2-3 Admit Reason.
  • Patient name is identifiable in the submissions: Per ASTP/ONC, for certification testing HIT developers must demonstrate that their system supports the Patient Name and pseudonymized data in PID-5 (this policy is conformant to the named standard HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019).The patient name presented in a pseudonymized manner is required for this Test Step.
  • Diagnoses are submitted test using either ICD-10-CM or SNOMED-CT: Per ASTP/ONC, for certification testing  a Health IT Module can use either the ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT® value sets in the submitted messages for all of the test steps in the Syndromic Surveillance Test Suite. Where the tool does not have test data that supports the Health IT Module’s value set (either ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT®), the developer of the Health IT Module must provide the codes and testers must perform a visual inspection of the messages for these test steps to ensure that equivalent and valid ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT® are used to populate the messages.  This policy is conformant to the named standard HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019). SNOMED-CT® codes are provided/required in the Test Data for coding the diagnoses in this Test Case.
  • Adult case where age is measured in years.
  • Addition of Height and Weight
  • Addition of Diagnoses
  • Update Diagnosis Post-Discharge
  • Insurance is unknown

Evaluation Criteria

No Evaluation Criteria


Notes

None


1.2.1. SS-UC-2.1_Registration_A04

Patient is registered at an Urgent Care Center and is assigned a working diagnosis.

Test Story

Description

62-year-old male walks into NIST Urgent Care on October 20, 2024 at 12:00 PM seeking care for gastrointestinal issues following exposure hurricane-related flood waters. The patient is registered by a clerical assistant who records the patient's name, date of birth, residence information, insurance information (PlanID123 from MyInsuranceCompany), race, ethnicity, smoking status as ex-smoker, and patient's chief complaint, which is “vomiting, diarrhea, nausea, and abdominal pain ". 

NIST Urgent Care is an independently operated outpatient facility that routinely sends electronic syndromic surveillance data to the State Department of Public Health (NIST PH SS FAC 2) in accordance with a state regulation. The state regulations permit sending identifiable patient information for the purposes of syndromic surveillance. At 12:15 PM, the facility`s electronic health record module for syndromic surveillance data assembles and transmits a Registration ADT message about this patient encounter. The "full, legal patient name" and birth date is included due to state required reporting of all patients, which is not always the case across all jurisdictions.  






Comments

Dates and times are provided in this test scenario to illustrate the sequence of clinical and messaging events. Since the exact dates and times are not reproducible when modeling the test scenario with HIT, only date and time format will be validated within tester submitted test data. 


Pre-condition

No Pre-Condition


Post-Condition

An ADT^A04 Registration message has been triggered.


Test Objectives

This test step examines an HIT Module's ability to create an ADT^A04 Registration message within HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019, conformance requirements.

The following test objectives and variants are supported by this test case:

  • Setting is Urgent Care.
  • Chief complaint is captured as a narrative in PV2-3 Admit Reason.
  • Patient name is identifiable in the submissions: Per ASTP/ONC, for certification testing HIT developers must demonstrate that their system supports the Patient Name and pseudonymized data in PID-5 (this policy is conformant to the named standard HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019).The patient name presented in an identified manner is required for this Test Step.
  • Adult case where age is measured in years.
  • Insurance is unknown

Evaluation Criteria

Message Validation Report


Notes

Messages in this test case will be used in a Manual Test Step under functional requirement testing.

1.2.2. SS-UC-2.2_Discharge_A03

Patient is discharged from Urgent Care Center and is assigned a final diagnosis.

Test Story

Description

At 12:30 PM, a nurse weighs the patient and measures his height and weight, which determines the patient BMI, and at 1pm. sets up an IV. This information is charted in the patient’s record to support the physician in ordering any medications. At 5:00 PM, the physician assigns the patient the reason for visit of ‘Noninfective gastroenteritis and colitis, unspecified’  and documents the information in the patient`s electronic medical record. The provider sends a stool sample to the lab for confirmation, and documents Home subject to flooding (finding) (SNOMED-CT 224259003)  and a working diagnosis of Gastroenteritis (disorder) (SNOMED-CT 25374005) in the patient record, and the patient is discharged home. The provider also documents admitting diagnoses of Dehydration (disorder) (SNOMED-CT 34095006),  and Nausea and vomiting (disorder) (SNOMED-CT 16932000).

NIST Urgent Care is an independently operated outpatient facility that routinely sends electronic syndromic surveillance data to the State Department of Public Health (NIST PH SS FAC 2) in accordance with state regulation. The state regulations permit sending identifiable patient information for the purposes of syndromic surveillance. in accordance with state regulation. At 6:00 PM on October 20, 2024, the facility`s electronic health record module for syndromic surveillance data assembles and transmits a Discharge ADT message about this patient encounter to the state. The "full, legal patient name" and birth date is included due to state required reporting of all patients, which is not always the case across all jurisdictions.  


Comments

Dates and times are provided in this test scenario to illustrate the sequence of clinical and messaging events. Since the exact dates and times are not reproducible when modeling the test scenario with HIT, only date and time format will be validated within tester submitted test data. The dates within the message and across the messages in this test case will be subject to visual inspection to verify data consistencies and the ability to conform to the requirement to send this message within 12 hours of the event triggering this message.


Pre-condition

A08-Update message is sent before the A03-Disposition message.


Post-Condition

No Post-Condition


Test Objectives

This test step examines a Health IT Module's ability to create an ADT^A03 Disposition message within HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019 conformance requirements.

The following test objectives and variants are supported by this test case:

  • Setting is Urgent Care.
  • Chief complaint is captured as narrative in PV2-3 Admit Reason.
  • Patient name is identifiable in the submissions: Per ASTP/ONC, for certification testing HIT developers must demonstrate that their system supports the Patient Name and pseudonymized data in PID-5 (this policy is conformant to the named standard HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019).The patient name presented in an identified manner is required for this Test Step.
  • Diagnoses are submitted test using either ICD-10-CM or SNOMED-CT: Per ASTP/ONC, for certification testing  a Health IT Module can use either the ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT® value sets in the submitted messages for all of the test steps in the Syndromic Surveillance Test Suite. Where the tool does not have test data that supports the Health IT Module’s value set (either ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT®), the developer of the Health IT Module must provide the codes and testers must perform a visual inspection of the messages for these test steps to ensure that equivalent and valid ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT® are used to populate the messages.  This policy is conformant to the named standard HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019). SNOMED-CT® codes are provided/required in the Test Data for coding the diagnoses in this Test Case.
  • Adult case where age is measured in years.
  • Addition of Height and Weight
  • Addition of Diagnoses
  • Insurance is unknown

Evaluation Criteria

The NIST column indicates what the NIST Tool Validation will accept in context-based testing (used for ONC Certification). A ‘Yes’ in the NIST column signifies that the tool will accept the format and not throw an error. A ‘No’ in the NIST column signifies that the tool will throw an error (specifically for DG1-3.3). However, based on the recommendation from the CDC SMEs and approval from ASTP/ONC the format is acceptable. The NIST test cases documents the instances where the test proctor can grant an exception when the tool issues and error.


Notes

SNOMED-CT and ICD-10 codes are provided/required in the Test Data for coding the diagnoses in this Test Case. If the HIT developer uses different but equivalent SNOMED-CT or ICD-10 CM codes than the ones provided, the Tester may ignore errors generated by the Test Tool related to incorrect code when the ICD-10 CM code data used in the message are determined via visual inspection to be valid data.

ICD-10 CM codes are acceptable with or without decimals.

Messages in this test case will be used in a Manual Test Step under functional requirement testing.

1.2.3. SS-UC-2.3_Update_A08

The clinician sees the patient and adds a working diagnosis, and procedure information.

Test Story

Description

The test results are available 4-days post-discharge confirming that the patient had a cryptosporidium infection. At 2PM, the physician reviews the test results and updates the patient's record with this new Cryptosporidiosis (disorder) diagnosis ((SNOMED-CT 240370009), and the working diagnosis 'Gastroenteritis (disorder)’ (SNOMED-CT 25374005) is updated as a final diagnosis.  

NIST Urgent Care is an independently operated outpatient facility that routinely sends electronic syndromic surveillance data to the State Department of Public Health (NIST PH SS FAC 2) in accordance with state regulation. At 6:00 PM, the facility`s electronic health record module for syndromic surveillance data assembles and transmits an Update ADT message with the working diagnoses and the height and weight information for this patient encounter to the State Public Health Department (NIST PH SS FAC 2). The "full, legal patient name" and birth date is included due to state required reporting of all patients, which is not always the case across all jurisdictions.  


Comments

Dates and times are provided in this test scenario to illustrate the sequence of clinical and messaging events. Since the exact dates and times are not reproducible when modeling the test scenario with HIT, only date and time format will be validated within tester submitted test data.


Pre-condition

A04-Registration and A03-Discharge messages are sent before the A08-Update message.


Post-Condition

Patient record is updated with procedures, diagnoses and patient height/weight. An ADT*A08 message has been generated with this updated content.


Test Objectives

This test step examines a Health IT Module's ability to create ADT^A08 Update message within HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019 conformance requirements.

The following test objectives and variants are supported by this test case:

  • Setting is Urgent Care.
  • Chief complaint is captured as narrative in PV2-3 Admit Reason.
  • Patient name is identifiable in the submissions: Per ASTP/ONC, for certification testing HIT developers must demonstrate that their system supports the Patient Name and pseudonymized data in PID-5 (this policy is conformant to the named standard HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019).The patient name presented in an identified manner is required for this Test Step.
  • Diagnoses are submitted test using either ICD-10-CM or SNOMED-CT: Per ASTP/ONC, for certification testing  a Health IT Module can use either the ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT® value sets in the submitted messages for all of the test steps in the Syndromic Surveillance Test Suite. Where the tool does not have test data that supports the Health IT Module’s value set (either ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT®), the developer of the Health IT Module must provide the codes and testers must perform a visual inspection of the messages for these test steps to ensure that equivalent and valid ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT® are used to populate the messages.  This policy is conformant to the named standard HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019). SNOMED-CT® codes are provided/required in the Test Data for coding the diagnoses in this Test Case.
  • Adult case where age is measured in years.
  • Update Diagnosis Post-Discharge
  • Insurance is unknown

Evaluation Criteria

The NIST column indicates what the NIST Tool Validation will accept in context-based testing (used for ONC Certification). A ‘Yes’ in the NIST column signifies that the tool will accept the format and not throw an error. A ‘No’ in the NIST column signifies that the tool will throw an error (specifically for DG1-3.3). However, based on the recommendation from the CDC SMEs and approval from ASTP/ONC the format is acceptable. The NIST test cases documents the instances where the test proctor can grant an exception when the tool issues and error.


Notes

SNOMED-CT and ICD-10 codes are provided/required in the Test Data for coding the diagnoses in this Test Case. If the HIT developer uses different but equivalent SNOMED-CT or ICD-10 CM codes than the ones provided, the Tester may ignore errors generated by the Test Tool related to incorrect code when the ICD-10 CM code data used in the message are determined via visual inspection to be valid data.

ICD-10 CM codes are acceptable with or without decimals.

Messages in this test case will be used in a Manual Test Step under functional requirement testing.

2.1. SS-ED-1_ED_Visit_Patient_Dies

Patient visits ED and dies of head injuries.

Test Story

Description

Syndromic surveillance is public health surveillance that emphasizes the use of near "real-time" health data and statistical tools for disease or hazardous event detection, situation awareness for mass gatherings and public health emergencies, and ad hoc and population health trend analyses. For syndromic surveillance purposes, required data on all clinical encounters must be sent to the jurisdictional public health agency within 12 hours of any given encounter, and in accordance with local or state laws and practices.
The ED Visit Test Case provides an example of an ED visit where the patient's demographic information is unavailable at registration, admit/encounter reason is captured as a coded value using an ICD-10 CM code, a working diagnosis and a final diagnosis are captured using ICD-10 CM codes, and the patient dies. This Test Case will test using a pseudonymized patient record.

In this test case, an unconscious, unknown female patient is brought to an emergency department by ambulance after she sustained critical head and neck injuries due to a collision with a bus while she was riding a bicycle. She is placed on a ventilator, diagnosed as having severe head trauma, and expires from her injuries. Syndromic surveillance data about the visit are transmitted to the state health department.


This test case involves 3 steps:

  • a registration message (ADT^A04),
  • an update message (ADT^A08), and
  • a discharge message (ADT^A03).

Messages in this test case will be used in a Manual Test Step under functional requirement testing for validating the HIT capability of populating Visit ID (PV1-19.1) and Visit Time (PV1-44.1) each with the same value for all messages included in this Test Case.


Comments

No Comments


Pre-condition

No Pre-condition


Post-Condition

No Post-Condition


Test Objectives

This test case examines a Health IT Module's ability to create an ADT^A04-Registration message, an ADT^A08-Update message, and an ADT^A03-Discharge message within HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019, conformance requirements.

The following test objectives and variants are supported by this test case:

  • Setting is Emergency Department.
  • Chief complaint is captured as narrative in PV2-3 Admit Reason.
  • Patient name is de-identified in the submissions: Per ASTP/ONC, for certification testing HIT developers must demonstrate that their system supports the Patient Name and de-identified data in PID-5 (this policy is conformant to the named standard HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019).The patient name presented in a de-identified manner is required for this Test Step.
  • Diagnoses are submitted test using either ICD-10-CM or SNOMED-CT: Per ASTP/ONC, for certification testing  a Health IT Module can use either the ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT® value sets in the submitted messages for all of the test steps in the Syndromic Surveillance Test Suite. Where the tool does not have test data that supports the Health IT Module’s value set (either ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT®), the developer of the Health IT Module must provide the codes and testers must perform a visual inspection of the messages for these test steps to ensure that equivalent and valid ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT® are used to populate the messages.  This policy is conformant to the named standard HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019). ICD-10-CM codes are provided/required in the Test Data for coding the diagnoses in this Test Case.
  • Adult case where age is measured in years.
  • Updates to patient demographics
  • Updates to Diagnoses
  • Discharge Disposition Expired
  • Insurance is known

Evaluation Criteria

No Evaluation Criteria


Notes

None


2.1.1. SS-ED-1.1_Registration_A04

Patient is registered in the ED.

Test Story

Description

An unconscious white female with critical injuries to her head and neck is brought by ambulance to NIST Hospital's Emergency Department at 5:00 PM on July 17, 2024. The paramedics report that the patient turned her bicycle in to a bus moving at 35 mph. The collision threw the cyclist, who was not wearing a helmet, head-first to the ground. The patient's vital signs are stable, but she is on ventilation. Unable to find any identification, the patient is registered without her true name, date of birth, race, ethnicity, insurance information, and health history. Her admit/encounter reason is logged as V13.4XXA (Pedal cycle driver injured in collision with car, pick-up truck or van in traffic accident, initial encounter). No working diagnoses are assigned. 

NIST Hospital reports syndromic surveillance data to the State Public Health Department (NIST PH SS FAC 2). At 5:15 PM on July 17, 2024, the hospital's electronic health record module for syndromic surveillance data assembles and transmits an ADT^A04 Registration message about this encounter. The patient name and birthdate are de-identified in the submission, so no date of birth is included, and the name is represented with name type 'S' to indicate that the name is empty for anonymity.


Comments

The dates and times in this test case illustrate the sequence of clinical and messaging events. Since the exact dates and times are not reproducible when modeling the test case with a Health IT Module, only date and time format will be validated within tester submitted test data.


Pre-condition

No Pre-Condition


Post-Condition

No Post-Condition


Test Objectives

This test step examines a HIT Module's ability to create ADT^A04 Registration message within HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019 conformance requirements.

The following test objectives and variants are supported by this test case:

  • Setting is Emergency Department.
  • Chief complaint is captured as narrative in PV2-3 Admit Reason.
  • Patient name is de-identified in the submissions: Per ASTP/ONC, for certification testing HIT developers must demonstrate that their system supports the Patient Name and de-identified data in PID-5 (this policy is conformant to the named standard HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019).The patient name presented in a de-identified manner is required for this Test Step.
  • Adult case where age is measured in years.
  • Insurance is known

Evaluation Criteria

Message Validation Report


Notes

ICD-10 CM codes are provided in the Test Data and required for coding the diagnoses in this Test Case. If the HIT developer uses different but equivalent ICD-10 CM codes than the ones provided, the Tester may ignore errors generated by the Test Tool related to incorrect code when the ICD-10 CM code data used in the message are determined via visual inspection to be valid data.

ICD-10 CM codes are acceptable with or without decimals.

Messages in this test case will be used in a Manual Test Step under functional requirement testing.

2.1.2. SS-ED-1.2_Update_A08

ED sends updated Syndromic Surveillance message to state health department.

Test Story

Description

Physical examination, CT scan of the head and neck (ICD-10 Procedure Code: BW290ZZ  Computerized Tomography (CT Scan) of Head and Neck using High Osmolar Contrast) reveal a fracture to the base of the patient's skull and massive intracranial bleeding. While the patient's examination and diagnostic tests are proceeding an emergency room tech uncovers the patient's driver's license among the belongings brought to the ED by the paramedics along with a flight stub showing she traveled from Paris 2 days earlier. At 5:20 PM the patient's medical record is updated with working diagnosis ICD-10 codes S02.112A (Type III occipital condyle fracture, initial encounter for closed fracture) and S06.9X3A (Unspecified intracranial injury with loss of consciousness of 1 hour to 5 hours 59 minutes, initial encounter). Also at this time, the patient's license is used to update the medical record with name and age. 

NIST Hospital's Emergenyt Depsartment reports syndromic surveillance data to the State Public Health Department (NIST PH SS FAC 2) at least once per day. At 5:30 PM on July 17, 2024, the hospital's electronic health record module for syndromic surveillance data assembles and transmits an ADT 08 Update message about this encounter. The patient name and birthdate are de-identified in the submission, so no date of birth is included, and the name is represented with name type 'S' to indicate that the name is empty for anonymity.


Comments

The dates and times in this test case illustrate the sequence of clinical and messaging events. Since the exact dates and times are not reproducible when modeling the test case with an HIT Module, only date and time format will be validated within tester submitted test data.


Pre-condition

A04-Registration message is sent before the A08-Update message.


Post-Condition

No Post-Condition


Test Objectives

This test step examines an HIT Module's ability to create ADT^A08 Update message within HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019 conformance requirements.

The following test objectives and variants are supported by this test case:

  • Setting is Emergency Department.
  • Chief complaint is captured as narrative in PV2-3 Admit Reason.
  • Patient name is de-identified in the submissions: Per ASTP/ONC, for certification testing HIT developers must demonstrate that their system supports the Patient Name and de-identified data in PID-5 (this policy is conformant to the named standard HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019).The patient name presented in a de-identified manner is required for this Test Step.
  • Diagnoses are submitted test using either ICD-10-CM or SNOMED-CT: Per ASTP/ONC, for certification testing  a Health IT Module can use either the ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT® value sets in the submitted messages for all of the test steps in the Syndromic Surveillance Test Suite. Where the tool does not have test data that supports the Health IT Module’s value set (either ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT®), the developer of the Health IT Module must provide the codes and testers must perform a visual inspection of the messages for these test steps to ensure that equivalent and valid ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT® are used to populate the messages.  This policy is conformant to the named standard HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019). ICD-10-CM codes are provided/required in the Test Data for coding the diagnoses in this Test Case.
  • Adult case where age is measured in years.
  • Updates to patient demographics
  • Addition of Diagnoses
  • Patient Expired
  • Insurance is known

Evaluation Criteria

Message Validation Report

Diagnosis code (DG1-3) validation is validated as follows:

The table below summarizes the format representations that are accepted for ONC Health IT Module Certification. Rows 1 and 2 show the formats as required by the implementation guide and standard terminology rules and best practices. The last 3 columns show acceptable alternative formats. For this test case one of the following values shall be present in the message:

The NIST column indicates what the NIST Tool Validation will accept in context-based testing (used for ONC Certification). A ‘Yes’ in the NIST column signifies that the tool will accept the format and not throw an error. A ‘No’ in the NIST column signifies that the tool will throw an error (specifically for DG1-3.3). However, based on the recommendation from the CDC SMEs and approval from ASTP/ONC the format is acceptable. The NIST test cases documents the instances where the test proctor can grant an exception when the tool issues and error.


Notes

ICD-10 CM codes are provided in the Test Data and required for coding the diagnoses in this Test Case. If the HIT developer uses different but equivalent ICD-10 CM codes than the ones provided, the Tester may ignore errors generated by the Test Tool related to incorrect code when the ICD-10 CM code data used in the message are determined via visual inspection to be valid data.

ICD-10 CM codes are acceptable with or without decimals.

Messages in this test case will be used in a Manual Test Step under functional requirement testing.

2.1.3. SS-ED-1.3_Discharge_A03

Expired patient is discharged (dispositioned).

Test Story

Description

At 5:41 PM, the patient dies. The working diagnoses (ICD-10-CM S02.112A and S06.9X3A) are resolved/inactivated, and the final diagnoses are S06.0X8A (Concussion with LOC of any duration with death due to other cause prior to regaining consciousness) and S02.112A (Type III occipital condyle fracture, initial encounter for closed fracture). 

NIST Hospital's Emergency Department reports syndromic surveillance data to the State Public Health Department (NIST PH SS FAC 2) at least once per day. At 5:50, the patient is discharged to the morgue. At 6:00 PM on July 17, 2024, the hospital's electronic health record module for syndromic surveillance data assembles and transmits an ADT^A03 Discharge message about this encounter. The patient name and birthdate are de-identified in the submission, so no date of birth is included, and the name is represented with name type 'S' to indicate that the name is empty for anonymity.


Comments

The dates and times in this test case illustrate the sequence of clinical and messaging events. Since the exact dates and times are not reproducible when modeling the test case with an HIT Module, only date and time format will be validated within tester submitted test data.


Pre-condition

A08-Update message is sent before the A03-Discharge message.


Post-Condition

No Post-Condition


Test Objectives

This test step examines an HIT Module's ability to create an ADT^A03 Disposition message within HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019 conformance requirements.

The following test objectives and variants are supported by this test case:

  • Setting is Emergency Department.
  • Chief complaint is captured as narrative in PV2-3 Admit Reason.
  • Patient name is de-identified in the submissions: Per ASTP/ONC, for certification testing HIT developers must demonstrate that their system supports the Patient Name and de-identified data in PID-5 (this policy is conformant to the named standard HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019).The patient name presented in a de-identified manner is required for this Test Step.
  • Diagnoses are submitted test using either ICD-10-CM or SNOMED-CT: Per ASTP/ONC, for certification testing  a Health IT Module can use either the ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT® value sets in the submitted messages for all of the test steps in the Syndromic Surveillance Test Suite. Where the tool does not have test data that supports the Health IT Module’s value set (either ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT®), the developer of the Health IT Module must provide the codes and testers must perform a visual inspection of the messages for these test steps to ensure that equivalent and valid ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT® are used to populate the messages.  This policy is conformant to the named standard HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019). ICD-10-CM codes are provided/required in the Test Data for coding the diagnoses in this Test Case.
  • Adult case where age is measured in years.
  • Discharge Disposition Expired
  • Insurance is known

Evaluation Criteria

Message Validation Report

The NIST column indicates what the NIST Tool Validation will accept in context-based testing (used for ONC Certification). A ‘Yes’ in the NIST column signifies that the tool will accept the format and not throw an error. A ‘No’ in the NIST column signifies that the tool will throw an error (specifically for DG1-3.3). However, based on the recommendation from the CDC SMEs and approval from ASTP/ONC the format is acceptable. The NIST test cases documents the instances where the test proctor can grant an exception when the tool issues and error.


Notes

ICD-10 CM codes are provided in the test data for coding the admit/encounter reason and final diagnoses. If the HIT developer uses different but equivalent ICD-10 CM codes than the ones provided or uses equivalent SNOMED CT codes, the Tester may ignore errors generated by the Test Tool related to incorrect code/name of coding system when the ICD-10 CM or SNOMED CT code data used in the message are determined via visual inspection to be valid data.

ICD-10 CM codes are acceptable with or without decimals.

This Test Step does not prescribe the method used by the Health IT Module to change a working diagnosis to a final diagnosis. 

Messages in this test case will be used in a Manual Test Step under functional requirement testing.

2.2. SS-ED-2_ED_Visit_Patient_Admitted

ED Visit with addmission for HBOT.

Test Story

Description

A woman arrives to the Southern Midwest Medical Center's Emergency Department in active labor, and delivers in the emergency department at 7:30 AM on February 1, 2025. The newborn has a Congenital diaphragmatic hernia (CDH), and has suffered Meconium aspiration syndrome (MAS). A clerical assistant registers the newborn as a patient. She records the date of birth, race, ethnicity, residence, insurance information, and health history. The baby has not yet been given a name and is subsequently updated once provided by the mother, The clerical assistant also enters the patient's chief complaint as, "Emergency Room birth with Congenital diaphragmatic hernia (CDH) (ICD-10 Q79. 0),". Upon examination, the physician determines the newborn should be admitted to the hospital NICU and put on ECMO to stabilize the baby before performing surgery to repair the CDH (0BQT0ZZ Repair Diaphragm, Open Approach). The newborn is discharged home after recovering from surgery. The Southern Midwest Medical Center reports syndromic surveillance data to the state health department (SHD). At 7:45 AM on February 1, 2025, the hospital's electronic health record module for syndromic surveillance data assembles and transmits a Registration message about this encounter to SHD.

This Test Case provides an example of an ED visit for which the patient's chief complaint is captured as free-text, working diagnosis and final diagnosis are captured with SNOMED CT codes, the patient is discharged from the ED and admitted for inpatient care, and the Admit/Encounter Reason is captured with a SNOMED CT code.

This test case involves 4 steps:

  • a registration message (ADT^A04),
  • an update message (ADT^A08),
  • a discharge message (ADT^A03)
  • an admit message (ADT^A01).

Messages in this test case will be used in a Manual Test Step under functional requirement testing for validating the HIT capability of populating Visit ID (PV1-19.1) and Visit Time (PV1-44.1) each with the same value for all messages included in this Test Case.


Comments

No Comments


Pre-condition

No PreCondition


Post-Condition

No PostCondition


Test Objectives

This test case examines a Health IT Module's ability to create an ADT^A04-Registration message, an ADT^A08-Update message, an ADT^A03-Discharge message, and an ADT^A01-Admission message within HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019, conformance requirements.

The following test objectives and variants are supported by this test case:

  • Setting is Emergency Department with Inpatient Admission.
  • Chief complaint is captured as narrative in PV2-3 Admit Reason.
  • Diagnoses are submitted test using either ICD-10-CM or SNOMED-CT: Per ASTP/ONC, for certification testing  a Health IT Module can use either the ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT® value sets in the submitted messages for all of the test steps in the Syndromic Surveillance Test Suite. Where the tool does not have test data that supports the Health IT Module’s value set (either ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT®), the developer of the Health IT Module must provide the codes and testers must perform a visual inspection of the messages for these test steps to ensure that equivalent and valid ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT® are used to populate the messages.  This policy is conformant to the named standard HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019). SNOMED-CT® codes are provided/required in the Test Data for coding the diagnoses in this Test Case.
  • Patient Name is Identifiable in the submissions: Per ASTP/ONC, for certification testing HIT developers must demonstrate that their system supports the Patient Name and pseudonymized data in PID-5 (this policy is conformant to the named standard HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019).The patient name presented in an identified manner is required for this Test Step.
  • Multiple Races.
  • Pediatric case where age is measured in months.
  • Updates to patient demographics
  • Updates to Diagnoses.
  • Discharge Disposition Inpatient Admission followed by Home.
  • Insurance is unknown

Evaluation Criteria

No evaluation criteria


Notes

No Note


2.2.1. SS-ED-2.1_Registration_A04

Patient is registered in the ED.

Test Story

Description

A woman arrives to the NIST Hospital's Emergency Department in active labor, and delivers in the emergency department  at 7:30 AM on February 1, 2025. The newborn has a Congenital diaphragmatic hernia (CDH), which is entered as a working diagnosis, and has suffered Meconium aspiration syndrome (MAS), which is entered as final diagnoses as they were identified upon delivery. At 7:35, a clerical assistant registers the newborn as a patient. She records the patient's date of birth, the height and weight taken at birth, race, ethnicity, residence, insurance information (PlanID123 from MyInsuranceCompany). There is no patient name for the newborn at the time of birth.

 Southern Midwest Medical Center reports syndromic surveillance data to the City Health Department (NIST PH SS FAC 1). At 7:45 AM on February 1, 2025, the hospital's electronic health record module for syndromic surveillance data assembles and transmits a Registration message about this encounter. The placeholder patient name and birth date is included due to state required reporting of all patients, which is not always the case across all jurisdictions.  







Comments

Dates and times are provided in this test case to illustrate the sequence of clinical and messaging events. Since the exact dates and times are not reproducible when modeling the Test Case with an HIT Module, only date and time format will be validated within tester submitted test data.


Pre-condition

No Pre-Condition


Post-Condition

No Post-Condition


Test Objectives

This test step examines an HIT Module's ability to create an ADT^A04 Registration message within HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019, conformance requirements.

The following test objectives and variants are supported by this test case:

  • Setting is Emergency Department with Inpatient Admission.
  • Chief complaint is captured as free-text.
  • Diagnoses are submitted test using either ICD-10-CM or SNOMED-CT: Per ASTP/ONC, for certification testing  a Health IT Module can use either the ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT® value sets in the submitted messages for all of the test steps in the Syndromic Surveillance Test Suite. Where the tool does not have test data that supports the Health IT Module’s value set (either ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT®), the developer of the Health IT Module must provide the codes and testers must perform a visual inspection of the messages for these test steps to ensure that equivalent and valid ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT® are used to populate the messages.  This policy is conformant to the named standard HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019). SNOMED-CT® codes are provided/required in the Test Data for coding the diagnoses in this Test Case.
  • Patient Name is Identifiable in the submissions: Per ASTP/ONC, for certification testing HIT developers must demonstrate that their system supports the Patient Name and pseudonymized data in PID-5 (this policy is conformant to the named standard HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019).The patient name presented in an identified manner is required for this Test Step.
  • Multiple Races.
  • Pediatric case where age is measured in months.
  • Inclusion of  Admitting Diagnoses.
  • Insurance is unknown

Evaluation Criteria

The NIST column indicates what the NIST Tool Validation will accept in context-based testing (used for ONC Certification). A ‘Yes’ in the NIST column signifies that the tool will accept the format and not throw an error. A ‘No’ in the NIST column signifies that the tool will throw an error (specifically for DG1-3.3). However, based on the recommendation from the CDC SMEs and approval from ASTP/ONC the format is acceptable. The NIST test cases documents the instances where the test proctor can grant an exception when the tool issues and error.


Notes

Messages in this test case will be used in a Manual Test Step under functional requirement testing.

2.2.2. SS-ED-2.2_Update_A08

ED sends updated Syndromic Surveillance message to state health department.

Test Story

Description

At 8am, an abdominal CT (CPT 74150 Computed tomography, abdomen; without contrast material) is taken to determine the extent of CDH. The mother has also selected a baby name, which is updated in the record. The Abdominal scan shows the newborn has Pericardial and diaphragmatic defect syndrome (724068001) which is entered as the final diagnosis.

NIST Hospital reports syndromic surveillance data to the City Health Department (NIST PH SS FAC 1). At 8:15 AM on February 1, 2025, the hospital's electronic health record module for syndromic surveillance data assembles and transmits an Update message about this encounter. The "full, legal patient name" and birth date is included due to state required reporting of all patients, which is not always the case across all jurisdictions.  


Comments

Dates and times are provided in this test case to illustrate the sequence of clinical and messaging events. Since the exact dates and times are not reproducible when modeling the Test Case with an HIT Module, only date and time format will be validated within tester submitted test data.


Pre-condition

A04-Registration message is sent before A08-Update message.


Post-Condition

No PostCondition


Test Objectives

This test step examines an HIT Module's ability to create ADT^A08 Update message within HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019 conformance requirements.

  • Setting is Emergency Department with Inpatient Admission.
  • Chief complaint is captured as narrative in PV2-3 Admit Reason.
  • Diagnoses are submitted test using either ICD-10-CM or SNOMED-CT: Per ASTP/ONC, for certification testing  a Health IT Module can use either the ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT® value sets in the submitted messages for all of the test steps in the Syndromic Surveillance Test Suite. Where the tool does not have test data that supports the Health IT Module’s value set (either ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT®), the developer of the Health IT Module must provide the codes and testers must perform a visual inspection of the messages for these test steps to ensure that equivalent and valid ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT® are used to populate the messages.  This policy is conformant to the named standard HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019). SNOMED-CT® codes are provided/required in the Test Data for coding the diagnoses in this Test Case.
  • Patient Name is Identifiable in the submissions: Per ASTP/ONC, for certification testing HIT developers must demonstrate that their system supports the Patient Name and pseudonymized data in PID-5 (this policy is conformant to the named standard HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019).The patient name presented in an identified manner is required for this Test Step.
  • Multiple Races.
  • Pediatric case where age is measured in months.
  • Updates to patient demographics
  • Addition of Diagnoses.
  • Insurance is unknown

Evaluation Criteria

The NIST column indicates what the NIST Tool Validation will accept in context-based testing (used for ONC Certification). A ‘Yes’ in the NIST column signifies that the tool will accept the format and not throw an error. A ‘No’ in the NIST column signifies that the tool will throw an error (specifically for DG1-3.3). However, based on the recommendation from the CDC SMEs and approval from ASTP/ONC the format is acceptable. The NIST test cases documents the instances where the test proctor can grant an exception when the tool issues and error.


Notes

Codes are provided in the test data for coding the diagnoses and procedures. The Tool is designed to accept those codes without generating an error. If the HIT developer uses a different but equivalent coded value within the same code system  than the ones provided/accepted, the Tester may ignore errors generated by the Test Tool related to incorrect code/name of coding system when the coded data used in the message are determined via visual inspection to be valid data.

Messages in this test case will be used in a Manual Test Step under functional requirement testing.

2.2.3. SS-ED-2.3_Discharge_A03

Patient is discharged (dispositioned) from ED in preparation for admission to hospital.

Test Story

Description

Upon review of the imaging results, the physician determines the newborn should be admitted to the hospital NICU and put on ECMO to stabilize the baby before performing surgery to repair the CDH (0BQT0ZZ Repair Diaphragm, Open Approach).

 The patient is quickly discharged from the ED and transported within the facility to the NICU . At 8:25 AM, ED staff complete the patient record and administratively discharge the patient from the ED. The working diagnosis is updated to final diagnosis. The patient's final diagnosis is (Pericardial and diaphragmatic defect syndrome (disorder) (SNOMED CT code 724068001)),  NIST Hospital reports syndromic surveillance data to the City Health Department (NIST PH SS FAC 1). At 8:30 AM on February 1, 2025, the hospital's electronic health record module for syndromic surveillance data assembles and transmits a Discharge message about this encounter. The "full, legal patient name" and birth date is included due to state required reporting of all patients, which is not always the case across all jurisdictions.  


Comments

Dates and times are provided in this test case to illustrate the sequence of clinical and messaging events. Since the exact dates and times are not reproducible when modeling the Test Case with an HIT Module, only date and time format will be validated within tester submitted test data.


Pre-condition

A08-Update message is sent before A03-Discharge message.


Post-Condition

No PostCondition


Test Objectives

This test step examines an HIT Module's ability to create an ADT^A03 Discharge message within HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019 conformance requirements.

  • Setting is Emergency Department with Inpatient Admission.
  • Chief complaint is captured as narrative in PV2-3 Admit Reason.
  • Diagnoses are submitted test using either ICD-10-CM or SNOMED-CT: Per ASTP/ONC, for certification testing  a Health IT Module can use either the ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT® value sets in the submitted messages for all of the test steps in the Syndromic Surveillance Test Suite. Where the tool does not have test data that supports the Health IT Module’s value set (either ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT®), the developer of the Health IT Module must provide the codes and testers must perform a visual inspection of the messages for these test steps to ensure that equivalent and valid ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT® are used to populate the messages.  This policy is conformant to the named standard HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019). SNOMED-CT® codes are provided/required in the Test Data for coding the diagnoses in this Test Case.
  • Patient Name is Identifiable in the submissions: Per ASTP/ONC, for certification testing HIT developers must demonstrate that their system supports the Patient Name and pseudonymized data in PID-5 (this policy is conformant to the named standard HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019).The patient name presented in an identified manner is required for this Test Step.
  • Multiple Races.
  • Pediatric case where age is measured in months.
  • Updates to Diagnoses.
  • Discharge Disposition Inpatient Admission.
  • Insurance is unknown

The NIST column indicates what the NIST Tool Validation will accept in context-based testing (used for ONC Certification). A ‘Yes’ in the NIST column signifies that the tool will accept the format and not throw an error. A ‘No’ in the NIST column signifies that the tool will throw an error (specifically for DG1-3.3). However, based on the recommendation from the CDC SMEs and approval from ASTP/ONC the format is acceptable. The NIST test cases documents the instances where the test proctor can grant an exception when the tool issues and error.



Notes

Codes are provided in the test data for coding the diagnoses and procedures. The Tool is designed to accept those codes without generating an error. If the HIT developer uses a different but equivalent coded value within the same code system  than the ones provided/accepted, the Tester may ignore errors generated by the Test Tool related to incorrect code/name of coding system when the coded data used in the message are determined via visual inspection to be valid data.

Messages in this test case will be used in a Manual Test Step under functional requirement testing.

2.2.4. SS-ED-2.4_Admission_A01

Patient is prepared for admission to hospital.

Test Story

Description

While the patient is prepared for transport within the hospital for ECMO, the clinical staffers complete an admission record. The Reason for admission is "The Abdominal scan shows the newborn has Pericardial and diaphragmatic defect syndrome.". The Admit Diagnosis is recorded as, "Congenital diaphragmatic hernia (CDH)". 

NIST Hospital reports syndromic surveillance data to the City Health Department (NIST PH SS FAC 1). At 8:35 AM on February 1, 2025, the hospitals electronic health record module for syndromic surveillance data assembles and transmits an Admission message about this encounter. The "full, legal patient name" and birth date is included due to state required reporting of all patients, which is not always the case across all jurisdictions.  


Comments

Dates and times are provided in this test case to illustrate the sequence of clinical and messaging events. Since the exact dates and times are not reproducible when modeling the Test Case with an HIT Module, only date and time format will be validated within tester submitted test data.


Pre-condition

A03-Discharge message is sent before A01-Admission message.


Post-Condition

No Post-Condition


Test Objectives

This test step examines a Health IT Module's ability to create ADT^A01 Admission message within the HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019 conformance requirements for syndromic surveillance.

  • Setting is Emergency Department with Inpatient Admission.
  • Chief complaint is captured as narrative in PV2-3 Admit Reason.
  • Diagnoses are submitted test using either ICD-10-CM or SNOMED-CT: Per ASTP/ONC, for certification testing  a Health IT Module can use either the ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT® value sets in the submitted messages for all of the test steps in the Syndromic Surveillance Test Suite. Where the tool does not have test data that supports the Health IT Module’s value set (either ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT®), the developer of the Health IT Module must provide the codes and testers must perform a visual inspection of the messages for these test steps to ensure that equivalent and valid ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT® are used to populate the messages.  This policy is conformant to the named standard HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019). SNOMED-CT® codes are provided/required in the Test Data for coding the diagnoses in this Test Case.
  • Patient Name is Identifiable in the submissions: Per ASTP/ONC, for certification testing HIT developers must demonstrate that their system supports the Patient Name and pseudonymized data in PID-5 (this policy is conformant to the named standard HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019).The patient name presented in an identified manner is required for this Test Step.
  • Multiple Races.
  • Pediatric case where age is measured in months.
  • Addition of Admitting Diagnoses.
  • Discharge Disposition Home.
  • Insurance is unknown

Evaluation Criteria

Message Validation Report

Diagnosis code (DG1-3) validation is validated as follows:
