The NIST column indicates what the NIST Tool Validation will accept in context-based testing (used for ONC Certification). A ‘Yes’ in the NIST column signifies that the tool will accept the format and not throw an error. A ‘No’ in the NIST column signifies that the tool will throw an error (specifically for DG1-3.3). However, based on the recommendation from the CDC SMEs and approval from ASTP/ONC the format is acceptable. The NIST test cases documents the instances where the test proctor can grant an exception when the tool issues and error.


Notes

PV1-2 may be populated with either "E" or "I".

An OBX segment in the A01 message provides the originating visit type information through Facility/Visit Type. The admit described in the ED_Visit_Admit Test Scenario originated in the emergency department; therefore, "261QE0002X^Emergency Care^HCPTNUCC" is in the OBX.5 field in the Example A01 Message. Per ONC, the OBX.5 field may be populated with "1021-5^Inpatient practice setting^HSLOC" for certification testing. The Tool performs automated validation to check for either of those values.

Codes are provided in the test data for coding the diagnoses and procedures. The Tool is designed to accept those codes without generating an error. If the HIT developer uses a different but equivalent coded value within the same code system  than the ones provided/accepted, the Tester may ignore errors generated by the Test Tool related to incorrect code/name of coding system when the coded data used in the message are determined via visual inspection to be valid data.

Messages in this test case will be used in a Manual Test Step under functional requirement testing.

3.1. SS-IP-1_Inpatient_Surgery_Mortality

Patient has cardiac surgery

Test Story

Description

Syndromic surveillance is public health surveillance that emphasizes the use of near "real-time" health data and statistical tools for disease or hazardous event detection, situation awareness for mass gatherings and public health emergencies, and ad hoc and population health trend analyses. For syndromic surveillance purposes, required data on all clinical encounters must be sent to the jurisdictional public health agency within 24 hours of the any given encounter, and in accordance with local or state laws and practices.

The IP Visit-Cardiac Surgery Test Case provides an example of an inpatient surgical visit for a patient with multiple diagnoses. In this scenario, syndromic surveillance data are sent to a state public health agency in accordance with guidelines recommended by the International Society for Disease Surveillance (i.e., once following admission and once following discharge). 

In this test case, a middle aged man is admitted for a pre-scheduled surgical removal of a coronary artery obstruction and insertion of stents, spends one night in the hospital for observation, and is discharged home.

This test case involves 2 steps: 

  • an admission message (ADT^A01),
  • a discharge message (ADT^A03).

Messages in this test case will be used in a Manual Test Step under functional requirement testing for validating the HIT capability of populating Visit ID (PV1-19.1) and Visit Time (PV1-44.1) each with the same value for all messages included in this Test Case.


Comments

No Comments


Pre-condition

No PreCondition


Post-Condition

No PostCondition


Test Objectives

This test case examines a Health IT Module's ability to create an ADT^A04-Registration message, and an ADT^A03-Discharge message within HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019, conformance requirements.

The following test objectives and variants are supported by this test case:

  • Setting is inpatient.
  • Chief complaint is captured as narrative in PV2-3 Admit Reason
  • Patient name is de-identified in the submissions: Per ASTP/ONC, for certification testing HIT developers must demonstrate that their system supports the Patient Name and de-identified data in PID-5 (this policy is conformant to the named standard HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019).The patient name presented in a pseudonymized manner is required for this Test Step.
  • Diagnoses are submitted test using either ICD-10-CM or SNOMED-CT: Per ASTP/ONC, for certification testing  a Health IT Module can use either the ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT® value sets in the submitted messages for all of the test steps in the Syndromic Surveillance Test Suite. Where the tool does not have test data that supports the Health IT Module’s value set (either ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT®), the developer of the Health IT Module must provide the codes and testers must perform a visual inspection of the messages for these test steps to ensure that equivalent and valid ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT® are used to populate the messages.  This policy is conformant to the named standard HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019). ICD-10-CM codes are provided/required in the Test Data for coding the diagnoses in this Test Case.
  • Updates to Diagnoses
  • Discharge Disposition Expired

Evaluation Criteria

No evaluation criteria


Notes

No Note


3.1.1. SS-IP-1.2_Admission_A01

Patient Admitted to Hospital for cardiac surgery

Test Story

Description

On June 17, 2025 at 7:00 AM, a 51-year old male arrives at NIST Hospital for a scheduled surgical removal of a coronary artery obstruction and insertion of stents (Dilation of 2 Cor Art with Intralum Dev, Open Approach (ICD-10-PCS 02710DZ) to treat his cardiac condition ( I25110 Atherosclerotic heart disease of native coronary artery with unstable angina pectoris ICD-10-CM).

The patient's name, date of birth, race, ethnicity, residence, and insurance information are captured and the patient is escorted to a pre-operation room where a nurse captures the patient's chief complaint as fatigue and shortness of breath, inputs the reason for visit as I25.110 (Atherosclerotic heart disease of native coronary artery with unstable angina pectoris), and documents angina pectoris for the patient's admitting diagnosis (I20.9).The patient is then prepared for the procedure, and at 8:30 AM the patient has the procedure. It occurs without incident, and the patient goes to post-operation recovery unit to wake from anesthesia, and receive a post-op examination. At 2:00 PM the patient, who is in good condition, is transported to a cardiac unit for overnight observation. 

NIST Hospital reports inpatient syndromic surveillance data to the State Public Health Department (NIST PH SS FAC 2) for all new patient admissions at least once per day. At 8:00 PM on June 17, 2025, the hospital's electronic health record module for syndromic surveillance data assembles and transmits an ADT^A01 Admission message about this encounter. The patient name and birthdate are de-identified in the submission, so no date of birth is included, and the name is represented with name type 'S' to indicate that the name is empty for anonymity.


Comments

The dates and times in this test case illustrate the sequence of clinical and messaging events. Since the exact dates and times are not reproducible when modeling the test case with EHR technology, only date and time format will be validated within tester submitted test data.


Pre-condition

A04-Registration message is sent before A01-Admisssion message.


Post-Condition

No PostCondition


Test Objectives

This test step examines a Health IT Module's ability to create ADT^A01 Admission message within PHIN Syndromic Surveillance Messaging Guide's conformance requirements.

  • Setting is inpatient.
  • Chief complaint is captured as narrative in PV2-3 Admit Reason
  • Patient name is de-identified in the submissions: Per ASTP/ONC, for certification testing HIT developers must demonstrate that their system supports the Patient Name and de-identified data in PID-5 (this policy is conformant to the named standard HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019).The patient name presented in a de-identified manner is required for this Test Step.
  • Diagnoses are submitted test using either ICD-10-CM or SNOMED-CT: Per ASTP/ONC, for certification testing  a Health IT Module can use either the ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT® value sets in the submitted messages for all of the test steps in the Syndromic Surveillance Test Suite. Where the tool does not have test data that supports the Health IT Module’s value set (either ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT®), the developer of the Health IT Module must provide the codes and testers must perform a visual inspection of the messages for these test steps to ensure that equivalent and valid ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT® are used to populate the messages.  This policy is conformant to the named standard HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019). ICD-10-CM codes are provided/required in the Test Data for coding the diagnoses in this Test Case.
  • Admitting Diagnoses

Evaluation Criteria

Message Validation Report

Diagnosis code (DG1-3) validation is validated as follows:

The table below summarizes the format representations that are accepted for ONC Health IT Module Certification. Rows 1 and 2 show the formats as required by the implementation guide and standard terminology rules and best practices. The last 3 columns show acceptable alternative formats. For this test case one of the following values shall be present in the message:

The NIST column indicates what the NIST Tool Validation will accept in context-based testing (used for ONC Certification). A ‘Yes’ in the NIST column signifies that the tool will accept the format and not throw an error. A ‘No’ in the NIST column signifies that the tool will throw an error (specifically for DG1-3.3). However, based on the recommendation from the CDC SMEs and approval from ASTP/ONC the format is acceptable. The NIST test cases documents the instances where the test proctor can grant an exception when the tool issues and error.


Notes

Codes are provided in the test data for coding the diagnoses and procedures. The Tool is designed to accept those codes without generating an error. If the HIT developer uses a different but equivalent coded value within the same code system  than the ones provided/accepted, the Tester may ignore errors generated by the Test Tool related to incorrect code/name of coding system when the coded data used in the message are determined via visual inspection to be valid data.

ICD-10 CM codes are acceptable with or without decimals.

Messages in this test case will be used in a Manual Test Step under functional requirement testing.

3.1.2. SS-ED-1.2_Update_A08

ED sends updated Syndromic Surveillance message to state health department.

Test Story

Description

At 9:00 AM the next day, the patient is visited by the surgeon and an attending physician who perform a physical to check for signs and symptoms of any post-operation compilations. The patient reports that he is experiencing chest pain. The physicians both observe that the patient has signs of cardiac arrhythmia (I49.9). However, the patient suddenly suffers a pulmonary embolism and dies at 12pm. The pulmonary embolism (ICD-10 I26 Pulmonary embolism) is documented as a final diagnosis.

NIST Hospital reports syndromic surveillance data to the State Public Health Department (NIST PH SS FAC 2) at least once per day. At 12:30 PM on July 17, 2024, the hospital's electronic health record module for syndromic surveillance data assembles and transmits an ADT 08 Update message about this encounter. The patient name and birthdate are de-identified in the submission, so no date of birth is included, and the name is represented with name type 'S' to indicate that the name is empty for anonymity.


Comments

The dates and times in this test case illustrate the sequence of clinical and messaging events. Since the exact dates and times are not reproducible when modeling the test case with an HIT Module, only date and time format will be validated within tester submitted test data.


Pre-condition

A04-Registration message is sent before the A08-Update message.


Post-Condition

No Post-Condition


Test Objectives

This test step examines an HIT Module's ability to create ADT^A08 Update message within HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019 conformance requirements.

  • Setting is inpatient.
  • Chief complaint is captured as narrative in PV2-3 Admit Reason
  • Patient name is de-identified in the submissions: Per ASTP/ONC, for certification testing HIT developers must demonstrate that their system supports the Patient Name and de-identified data in PID-5 (this policy is conformant to the named standard HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019).The patient name presented in a de-identified manner is required for this Test Step.
  • Diagnoses are submitted test using either ICD-10-CM or SNOMED-CT: Per ASTP/ONC, for certification testing  a Health IT Module can use either the ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT® value sets in the submitted messages for all of the test steps in the Syndromic Surveillance Test Suite. Where the tool does not have test data that supports the Health IT Module’s value set (either ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT®), the developer of the Health IT Module must provide the codes and testers must perform a visual inspection of the messages for these test steps to ensure that equivalent and valid ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT® are used to populate the messages.  This policy is conformant to the named standard HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019). ICD-10-CM codes are provided/required in the Test Data for coding the diagnoses in this Test Case.
  • Addition of Diagnoses
  • Patient Expired

Evaluation Criteria

The NIST column indicates what the NIST Tool Validation will accept in context-based testing (used for ONC Certification). A ‘Yes’ in the NIST column signifies that the tool will accept the format and not throw an error. A ‘No’ in the NIST column signifies that the tool will throw an error (specifically for DG1-3.3). However, based on the recommendation from the CDC SMEs and approval from ASTP/ONC the format is acceptable. The NIST test cases documents the instances where the test proctor can grant an exception when the tool issues and error.


Notes

ICD-10 CM codes are provided in the Test Data and required for coding the diagnoses in this Test Case. If the HIT developer uses different but equivalent ICD-10 CM codes than the ones provided, the Tester may ignore errors generated by the Test Tool related to incorrect code when the ICD-10 CM code data used in the message are determined via visual inspection to be valid data.

ICD-10 CM codes are acceptable with or without decimals.

Messages in this test case will be used in a Manual Test Step under functional requirement testing.

3.1.3. SS-IP-1.3_Discharge_A03

Patient Discharged from Hospital to Home

Test Story

Description

At 12:45pm, the body is moved from the inpatient floor to the hospital morgue.  The unit coordinator enters the date/time the body leaves the floor as the discharge date and time.

The hospital's electronic health record module for syndromic surveillance data assembles and transmits an ADT^A03 Discharge message about this encounter to the State Public Health Department (NIST PH SS FAC 2).


NIST Hospital reports inpatient syndromic surveillance data to the State Public Health Department for all new patient discharges at least once per day. At 8:00 PM on June 18, 2025, the hospital's electronic health record module for syndromic surveillance data assembles and transmits an ADT^A03 Discharge message about this encounter to State Public Health Department (NIST PH SS FAC 2). The patient name and birthdate are de-identified in the submission, so no date of birth is included, and the name is represented with name type 'S' to indicate that the name is empty for anonymity.


Comments

The dates and times in this test case illustrate the sequence of clinical and messaging events. Since the exact dates and times are not reproducible when modeling the test case with EHR technology, only date and time format will be validated within tester submitted test data.


Pre-condition

A01-Admission message is sent before A03-Discharge message.


Post-Condition

No PostCondition


Test Objectives

This test step examines a Health IT Module's ability to create ADT^A03 Discharge message within PHIN Messaging Guide's conformance requirements.

  • Setting is inpatient.
  • Chief complaint is captured as narrative in PV2-3 Admit Reason
  • Patient name is de-identified in the submissions: Per ASTP/ONC, for certification testing HIT developers must demonstrate that their system supports the Patient Name and de-identified data in PID-5 (this policy is conformant to the named standard HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019).The patient name presented in a de-identified manner is required for this Test Step.
  • Diagnoses are submitted test using either ICD-10-CM or SNOMED-CT: Per ASTP/ONC, for certification testing  a Health IT Module can use either the ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT® value sets in the submitted messages for all of the test steps in the Syndromic Surveillance Test Suite. Where the tool does not have test data that supports the Health IT Module’s value set (either ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT®), the developer of the Health IT Module must provide the codes and testers must perform a visual inspection of the messages for these test steps to ensure that equivalent and valid ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT® are used to populate the messages.  This policy is conformant to the named standard HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019). ICD-10-CM codes are provided/required in the Test Data for coding the diagnoses in this Test Case.
  • Discharge Disposition Expired

Evaluation Criteria

Message Validation Report

Diagnosis code (DG1-3) validation is validated as follows:

Rows 1 and 2 show the formats as required by the implementation guide and standard terminology rules and best practices. The last 3 columns show acceptable alternative formats. The NIST column indicates what the NIST Tool Validation will accept in context-based testing (used for ONC Certification). A ‘Yes’ in the NIST column signifies that the tool will accept the format and not throw an error. A ‘No’ in the NIST column signifies that the tool will throw an error (specifically for DG1-3.3). However, based on the recommendation from the CDC SMEs and approval from ASTP/ONC the format is acceptable. The NIST test cases documents the instances where the test proctor can grant an exception when the tool issues and error.


Notes

Codes are provided in the test data for coding the diagnoses and procedures. The Tool is designed to accept those codes without generating an error. If the HIT developer uses a different but equivalent coded value within the same code system  than the ones provided/accepted, the Tester may ignore errors generated by the Test Tool related to incorrect code/name of coding system when the coded data used in the message are determined via visual inspection to be valid data.

ICD-10 CM codes are acceptable with or without decimals.

Messages in this test case will be used in a Manual Test Step under functional requirement testing.

3.2. SS-IP-2_Inpatient_Visit_Pediatric

This is an inpatient pediatric medical diagnosis visit and treatment for an infant that presents with respiratory symptoms.

Test Story

Description

Syndromic surveillance is public health surveillance that emphasizes the use of near "real-time" health data and statistical tools for disease or hazardous event detection, situation awareness for mass gatherings and public health emergencies, and ad hoc and population health trend analyses. For Syndromic surveillance purposes, required data on all clinical encounters must be sent to the jurisdictional public health agency within 24 hours of the any given encounter, and in accordance with local or state laws and practices.

The IP – Pediatric Medical Diagnosis Test Case provides an example of an inpatient pediatric medical diagnosis visit and treatment for an infant that presents with respiratory symptoms. In this scenario, Syndromic surveillance data are sent to a state public health agency in accordance with guidelines recommended by the International Society for Disease Surveillance (i.e., once following admission, once for updates, and once following discharge).

In this test case, an 8-week old infant is admitted with severe respiratory symptoms, including runny nose, coughing, sneezing, fever, decreased appetite, wheezing and apnea . The infant is treated with Oxygen Therapy and IV Fluids . After reviewing PCR laboratory results, the pediatrician updates the patient record with a diagnosis of Respiratory Syncytial Virus Infection. Following recovery, the infant is discharged home.

This test case involves 3 steps: 

  • an admission message (ADT^A01),
  • an update message (ADT^A08), 
  • a discharge message (ADT^A03),
  • An update message post-discharge (ADT^A08).

Messages in this test case will be used in a Manual Test Step under functional requirement testing for validating the HIT capability of populating Visit ID (PV1-19.1) and Visit Time (PV1-44.1) each with the same value for all messages included in this Test Case.


Comments

No Comments


Pre-condition

No PreCondition


Post-Condition

No PostCondition


Test Objectives

This test case examines a Health IT Module's ability to create an ADT^A04-Registration message, an ADT^A08-Update message, and an ADT^A03-Discharge message within HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019, conformance requirements.

The following test objectives and variants are supported by this test case:

  • Setting is inpatient.
  • Chief complaint is captured as narrative in PV2-3 Admit Reason
  • Patient Name is Identifiable in the submissions: Per ASTP/ONC, for certification testing HIT developers must demonstrate that their system supports the Patient Name and pseudonymized data in PID-5 (this policy is conformant to the named standard HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019).The patient name presented in an identified manner is required for this Test Step.
  • Diagnoses are submitted test using either ICD-10-CM or SNOMED-CT: Per ASTP/ONC, for certification testing  a Health IT Module can use either the ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT® value sets in the submitted messages for all of the test steps in the Syndromic Surveillance Test Suite. Where the tool does not have test data that supports the Health IT Module’s value set (either ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT®), the developer of the Health IT Module must provide the codes and testers must perform a visual inspection of the messages for these test steps to ensure that equivalent and valid ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT® are used to populate the messages.  This policy is conformant to the named standard HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019). SNOMED-CT® codes are provided/required in the Test Data for coding the diagnoses in this Test Case.
  • Pediatric case where age is measured in months.
  • Updates to Diagnoses
  • Discharge Disposition Home
  • Multiple Races

Evaluation Criteria

No evaluation criteria


Notes

No Note


3.2.1. SS-IP-2.1_Admission_A01

Patient Admitted to Hospital for respiratory illness

Test Story

Description

On February 15, 2025 at 7:00 AM, an 8-week old infant is admitted to NIST Hospital with severe respiratory symptoms, including cough, fever, decreased appetite, wheezing and apnea. The infant’s pediatrician has ordered the admission, suspecting the child may have contracted Respiratory Syncytial Virus  (RSV). A clerical assistant registers the patient for admission. He records the patient's name, date of birth, race, ethnicity, residence, and insurance information. The triage nurse documents the admitting diagnoses as indicated by the pediatrician as:

56018004^Wheezing (finding)

386661006^Fever (finding)^SCT

724229002^Apnea of infancy (disorder)

700213005^Avoidant restrictive food intake disorder (disorder)

417850002^Respiratory tract congestion and cough (disorder)


NIST Hospital reports inpatient syndromic surveillance data to the State Department of Public Health (NIST PH SS FAC 2) for all new patient admissions at least at least once per day. At 7:15 AM on February 15, 2025, the hospital's electronic health record module for syndromic surveillance data assembles and transmits an ADT^A01 Admission message about this encounter. The "full, legal patient name" and birth date is included due to state required reporting of all patients, which is not always the case across all jurisdictions.  


Comments

The dates and times in this test case illustrate the sequence of clinical and messaging events. Since the exact dates and times are not reproducible when modeling the test case with EHR technology, only date and time format will be validated within tester submitted test data.


Pre-condition

No Pre-Condition


Post-Condition

No Post-Condition


Test Objectives

This test step examines a Health IT Module's ability to create ADT^A01 Admission message within HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019 conformance requirements.

  • Setting is inpatient.
  • Chief complaint is captured as narrative in PV2-3 Admit Reason
  • Patient Name is Identifiable in the submissions: Per ASTP/ONC, for certification testing HIT developers must demonstrate that their system supports the Patient Name and pseudonymized data in PID-5 (this policy is conformant to the named standard HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019).The patient name presented in an identified manner is required for this Test Step.
  • Diagnoses are submitted test using either ICD-10-CM or SNOMED-CT: Per ASTP/ONC, for certification testing  a Health IT Module can use either the ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT® value sets in the submitted messages for all of the test steps in the Syndromic Surveillance Test Suite. Where the tool does not have test data that supports the Health IT Module’s value set (either ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT®), the developer of the Health IT Module must provide the codes and testers must perform a visual inspection of the messages for these test steps to ensure that equivalent and valid ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT® are used to populate the messages.  This policy is conformant to the named standard HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019). SNOMED-CT® codes are provided/required in the Test Data for coding the diagnoses in this Test Case.
  • Pediatric case where age is measured in months.
  • Admitting Diagnoses
  • Multiple Races

The NIST column indicates what the NIST Tool Validation will accept in context-based testing (used for ONC Certification). A ‘Yes’ in the NIST column signifies that the tool will accept the format and not throw an error. A ‘No’ in the NIST column signifies that the tool will throw an error (specifically for DG1-3.3). However, based on the recommendation from the CDC SMEs and approval from ASTP/ONC the format is acceptable. The NIST test cases documents the instances where the test proctor can grant an exception when the tool issues and error.


Notes

Codes are provided in the test data for coding the diagnoses and procedures. The Tool is designed to accept those codes without generating an error. If the HIT developer uses a different but equivalent coded value within the same code system than the ones provided/accepted, the Tester may ignore errors generated by the Test Tool related to incorrect code/name of coding system when the coded data used in the message are determined via visual inspection to be valid data.

Messages in this test case will be used in a Manual Test Step under functional requirement testing.

3.2.2. SS-IP-2.2_Update_A08

The Hospital sends updated Syndromic Surveillance message to state health department.

Test Story

Description

The infant is treated with Oxygen Therapy (Extracorporeal Hyperbaric Oxygenation, Continuous ICD-10-PCS 5A05221) and IV Fluids (Introduce Electrol/Water Bal in Periph Vein, Perc ICD-10-PCS 3E0337Z.) After reviewing PCR laboratory results on February 16, 2025 at 1pm, the pediatrician updates the patient record with a diagnosis of Respiratory Syncytial Virus (RSV) Infection, documenting the updated diagnosis at 1pm. 

NIST Hospital reports inpatient syndromic surveillance data to the State Public Health Department (NIST PH SS FAC 2) for all new patient admissions at least once per day. At 1:15 PM on February 16, 2015, the hospital's electronic health record module for syndromic surveillance data assembles and transmits an ADT^A08 Update message about this encounter. The "full, legal patient name" and birth date is included due to state required reporting of all patients, which is not always the case across all jurisdictions.  


Comments

Dates and times are provided in this test case to illustrate the sequence of clinical and messaging events. Since the exact dates and times are not reproducible when modeling the Test Case with a Health IT Module, only date and time format will be validated within tester submitted test data.


Pre-condition

A01-Admission message is sent before A08-Update message.


Post-Condition

No Post-Condition


Test Objectives

This test step examines a Health IT Module's ability to create ADT^A08 Update message within HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019 conformance requirements

  • Setting is inpatient.
  • Chief complaint is captured as narrative in PV2-3 Admit Reason
  • Patient Name is Identifiable in the submissions: Per ASTP/ONC, for certification testing HIT developers must demonstrate that their system supports the Patient Name and pseudonymized data in PID-5 (this policy is conformant to the named standard HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019).The patient name presented in an identified manner is required for this Test Step.
  • Diagnoses are submitted test using either ICD-10-CM or SNOMED-CT: Per ASTP/ONC, for certification testing  a Health IT Module can use either the ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT® value sets in the submitted messages for all of the test steps in the Syndromic Surveillance Test Suite. Where the tool does not have test data that supports the Health IT Module’s value set (either ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT®), the developer of the Health IT Module must provide the codes and testers must perform a visual inspection of the messages for these test steps to ensure that equivalent and valid ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT® are used to populate the messages.  This policy is conformant to the named standard HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019). SNOMED-CT® codes are provided/required in the Test Data for coding the diagnoses in this Test Case.
  • Pediatric case where age is measured in months.
  • Updates to Diagnoses
  • Multiple Races

Evaluation Criteria

Message Validation Report

The NIST column indicates what the NIST Tool Validation will accept in context-based testing (used for ONC Certification). A ‘Yes’ in the NIST column signifies that the tool will accept the format and not throw an error. A ‘No’ in the NIST column signifies that the tool will throw an error (specifically for DG1-3.3). However, based on the recommendation from the CDC SMEs and approval from ASTP/ONC the format is acceptable. The NIST test cases documents the instances where the test proctor can grant an exception when the tool issues and error.


Notes

Codes are provided in the test data for coding the diagnoses and procedures. The Tool is designed to accept those codes without generating an error. If the HIT developer uses a different but equivalent coded value within the same code system  than the ones provided/accepted, the Tester may ignore errors generated by the Test Tool related to incorrect code/name of coding system when the coded data used in the message are determined via visual inspection to be valid data.

Messages in this test case will be used in a Manual Test Step under functional requirement testing.

3.2.3. SS-IP-2.3_Discharge_A03

Patient Discharged from Hospital to Home

Test Story

Description

Following recovery, the infant is discharged home on February 20, 2025 at 5pm. 

NIST Hospital reports inpatient syndromic surveillance data to the State Public Health Department (NIST PH SS FAC 2) for all new patient admissions at least once per day. At 5:15 PM on February 20, 2015, the hospital's electronic health record module for syndromic surveillance data assembles and transmits an ADT^A03 Discharge message about this encounter. The "full, legal patient name" and birth date is included due to state required reporting of all patients, which is not always the case across all jurisdictions.  


Comments

The dates and times in this test case illustrate the sequence of clinical and messaging events. Since the exact dates and times are not reproducible when modeling the test case with EHR technology, only date and time format will be validated within tester submitted test data.


Pre-condition

An A01-Admission message and an A08 Update message is sent before A03-Discharge message.


Post-Condition

No Post-Condition


Test Objectives

This test step examines a Health IT Module's ability to create ADT^A03 Discharge  message within HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019 conformance requirements.

  • Setting is inpatient.
  • Chief complaint is captured as narrative in PV2-3 Admit Reason
  • Patient Name is Identifiable in the submissions: Per ASTP/ONC, for certification testing HIT developers must demonstrate that their system supports the Patient Name and pseudonymized data in PID-5 (this policy is conformant to the named standard HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019).The patient name presented in an identified manner is required for this Test Step.
  • Diagnoses are submitted test using either ICD-10-CM or SNOMED-CT: Per ASTP/ONC, for certification testing  a Health IT Module can use either the ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT® value sets in the submitted messages for all of the test steps in the Syndromic Surveillance Test Suite. Where the tool does not have test data that supports the Health IT Module’s value set (either ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT®), the developer of the Health IT Module must provide the codes and testers must perform a visual inspection of the messages for these test steps to ensure that equivalent and valid ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT® are used to populate the messages.  This policy is conformant to the named standard HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019). SNOMED-CT® codes are provided/required in the Test Data for coding the diagnoses in this Test Case.
  • Pediatric case where age is measured in months.
  • Updates to Diagnoses
  • Discharge Disposition Home
  • Multiple Races

Evaluation Criteria

Message Validation Report

Diagnosis code (DG1-3) validation is validated as follows:

The NIST column indicates what the NIST Tool Validation will accept in context-based testing (used for ONC Certification). A ‘Yes’ in the NIST column signifies that the tool will accept the format and not throw an error. A ‘No’ in the NIST column signifies that the tool will throw an error (specifically for DG1-3.3). However, based on the recommendation from the CDC SMEs and approval from ASTP/ONC the format is acceptable. The NIST test cases documents the instances where the test proctor can grant an exception when the tool issues and error.


Notes

Codes are provided in the test data for coding the diagnoses and procedures. The Tool is designed to accept those codes without generating an error. If the HIT developer uses a different but equivalent coded value within the same code system  than the ones provided/accepted, the Tester may ignore errors generated by the Test Tool related to incorrect code/name of coding system when the coded data used in the message are determined via visual inspection to be valid data.

Messages in this test case will be used in a Manual Test Step under functional requirement testing.

4. Ambulatory Care Visit

Patient is seen in an Ambulatory Care Setting and is discharged/dispositioned to home.

Test Story

Description
null

Comments
null

null

null

null

Evaluation Criteria

Notes
null

4.1. SS-AC-1_Visit_AmbulatoryMeasles_Visit_Child

Test case addresses a child brought to their pediatrician with a rash, fever, cough, and watery eyes.

Test Story

Description

Syndromic surveillance is public health surveillance that emphasizes the use of near "real-time" health data and statistical tools for disease or hazardous event detection, situation awareness for mass gatherings and public health emergencies, and ad hoc and population health trend analyses. For syndromic surveillance purposes, required data on all clinical encounters must be sent to the jurisdictional public health agency within 12 hours of any given encounter, and in accordance with local or state laws and practices.


The Ambulatory Visit – PCP New Diagnosis Test Case provides an example of an encounter that takes place in the ambulatory setting. This test case also provides an example where the facility sending the syndromic surveillance message is different than the facility where the patient received care. This Test Case will test using ICD-10 Dx codes and pseudonymized patient name. In this test case, a pediatric patient is seen by his Pediatrician for a rash with a high fever, cough, and watery eyes . The family has recently returned from travel to an amusement park. The pediatrician suspects measles and orders a Real-time RT-PCR to confirm, and provides instruction for home isolation to prevent potential spread. Results received post-discharge are positive for measles, and the patient record is updated with the final diagnosis. Syndromic surveillance data about the visit are transmitted to the local health department.

This test case involves 3 steps: 

  • a registration message (ADT^A04), 
  • followed by a discharge message (ADT^A03), 
  • followed by an update message (ADT^A08)

Messages in this test case will be used in a Manual Test Step under functional requirement testing for validating the HIT capability of populating Visit ID (PV1-19.1) and Visit Time (PV1-44.1) each with the same value for all messages included in this Test Case.










Comments

No Comments


Pre-condition

No Pre-Condition


Post-Condition

No Post-Condition


Test Objectives

This test case examines a Health IT Module's ability to create an ADT^A04-Registration message, an ADT^A08-Update message, and an ADT^A03-Discharge message within HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019, conformance requirements.

The following test objectives and variants are supported by this test case:

  • Setting is Ambulatory.
  • Chief complaint is captured as narrative in PV2-3 Admit Reason
  • Patient name is de-identified in the submissions: Per ASTP/ONC, for certification testing HIT developers must demonstrate that their system supports the Patient Name and de-identified data in PID-5 (this policy is conformant to the named standard HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019).The patient name presented in a de-identifiedmanner is required for this Test Step.
  • Diagnoses are submitted test using either ICD-10-CM or SNOMED-CT: Per ASTP/ONC, for certification testing  a Health IT Module can use either the ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT® value sets in the submitted messages for all of the test steps in the Syndromic Surveillance Test Suite. Where the tool does not have test data that supports the Health IT Module’s value set (either ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT®), the developer of the Health IT Module must provide the codes and testers must perform a visual inspection of the messages for these test steps to ensure that equivalent and valid ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT® are used to populate the messages.  This policy is conformant to the named standard HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019). ICD-10-CM codes are provided/required in the Test Data for coding the diagnoses in this Test Case.
  • Pediatric case where age is measured in months.
  • Update Diagnosis Post-Discharge
  • Multiple Races
  • Insurance unknown

Evaluation Criteria

No Evaluation Criteria


Notes

None


4.1.1. SS-AC-1.1_Registration_A04

Patient is registered at an Ambulatory Care Center.

Test Story

Description

A mother brings her 10-month old male infant to see his physician at NIST Primary Care on February 20, 2025, at 8:30 AM. A clerical assistant registers the patient. She records the patient`s name, date of birth, race, ethnicity, residence, insurance information and health history. The clerical assistant also records the patient's chief complaint in free-text as, “Patient has a rash, high fever, cough, and watery eyes.” The mother also reports that they returned 8 days prior to the visit from travel to an amusement park which the clerk also includes in the record.  

NIST Primary Care is an outpatient facility that routinely sends electronic syndromic surveillance data to the City Health Department (NIST PH SS FAC 1) in accordance with a city regulation.  At 10:00 AM, the facility`s electronic health record module for syndromic surveillance data assembles and transmits a Registration ADT message about this patient encounter. The patient name and birthdate are de-identified in the submission, so no date of birth is included, and the name is represented with name type 'S' to indicate that the name is empty for anonymity.


Comments

The dates and times in this test case illustrate the sequence of clinical and messaging events. Since the exact dates and times are not reproducible when modeling the test case with EHR technology, only date and time format will be validated within tester submitted test data.

The dates within the message and across the messages in this test case will be subject to visual inspection to verify data consistencies and the ability to conform to the requirement to send this message within 12 hours of the event triggering this message.


Pre-condition

No Pre-Condition


Post-Condition

An ADT^A04 Registration message has been triggered.


Test Objectives

This test step examines an HIT Module's ability to create an ADT^A04 Registration message within HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019, conformance requirements.

  • Setting is Ambulatory.
  • Chief complaint is captured as narrative in PV2-3 Admit Reason
  • Patient name is de-identified in the submissions: Per ASTP/ONC, for certification testing HIT developers must demonstrate that their system supports the Patient Name and de-identified data in PID-5 (this policy is conformant to the named standard HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019).The patient name presented in a de-identifiedmanner is required for this Test Step.
  • Pediatric case where age is measured in months.
  • Multiple Races
  • Insurance unknown

Evaluation Criteria

Message Validation Report


Notes

Messages in this test case will be used in a Manual Test Step under functional requirement testing.

4.1.2. SS-AC-1.2_Discharge_A03

The clinician sees the patient and adds a working diagnosis, final assessment diagnoses, demographic updates, and procedure information, discharging the patient to home.

Test Story

Description

The pediatrician suspects measles and orders a Real-time RT-PCR to confirm, and provides instruction for home isolation to prevent potential spread.

A nurse weighs the patient, measures his length, and charts the data in the patient’s record at 8:35. This information may be used by the physician if ordering the take-home prescription.  At 8:55 AM, the physician assigns the patient the working diagnoses of measles: (ICD-10 CM diagnosis code of Measles with intestinal complication B05.4), Conjunctivitis (disorder) SNOMED-CT 9826008), Fever, unspecified (SNOMED-CT 426000000), and Hacking cough (finding)  (SNOMED-CT 59994004)  are documented as final in the patient`s electronic medical record. Suspecting Measles, the provider takes a nasal swab to send for Real-time RT-PCR. The provider recommends isolation at home, providing education to the parents.  Education about isolation for infection control (procedure) (SNOMED-CT 737612005). The visit is closed at 9:05 am after the nurse collects the sample to send to the lab.  

NIST Primary Care is an outpatient facility that routinely sends electronic syndromic surveillance data to the City Health Department (NIST PH SS FAC 1) in accordance with a city regulation. At 6:00 PM, the facility`s electronic health record module for syndromic surveillance data assembles and transmits a Discharge ADT message with height and weight information, as well as the working and final diagnoses for this patient encounter. The patient name and birthdate are de-identified in the submission, so no date of birth is included, and the name is represented with name type 'S' to indicate that the name is empty for anonymity.













Comments

The dates and times in this test case illustrate the sequence of clinical and messaging events. Since the exact dates and times are not reproducible when modeling the test case with EHR technology, only date and time format will be validated within tester submitted test data.

The dates within the message and across the messages in this test case will be subject to visual inspection to verify data consistencies and the ability to conform to the requirement to send this message within 12 hours of the event triggering this message.


Pre-condition

A04-Registration message is sent before the A03-Discharge message.


Post-Condition

An A03-Discharge message has been triggered.


Test Objectives

This test step examines a Health IT Module's ability to create an ADT^A03 Disposition message within HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019 conformance requirements.

  • Setting is Ambulatory.
  • Chief complaint is captured as narrative in PV2-3 Admit Reason
  • Patient name is de-identified in the submissions: Per ASTP/ONC, for certification testing HIT developers must demonstrate that their system supports the Patient Name and de-identified data in PID-5 (this policy is conformant to the named standard HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019).The patient name presented in a de-identifiedmanner is required for this Test Step.
  • Diagnoses are submitted test using either ICD-10-CM or SNOMED-CT: Per ASTP/ONC, for certification testing  a Health IT Module can use either the ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT® value sets in the submitted messages for all of the test steps in the Syndromic Surveillance Test Suite. Where the tool does not have test data that supports the Health IT Module’s value set (either ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT®), the developer of the Health IT Module must provide the codes and testers must perform a visual inspection of the messages for these test steps to ensure that equivalent and valid ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT® are used to populate the messages.  This policy is conformant to the named standard HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019). ICD-10-CM codes are provided/required in the Test Data for coding the diagnoses in this Test Case.
  • Pediatric case where age is measured in months.
  • Add Diagnoses
  • Multiple Races
  • Insurance unknown

Evaluation Criteria

The NIST column indicates what the NIST Tool Validation will accept in context-based testing (used for ONC Certification). A ‘Yes’ in the NIST column signifies that the tool will accept the format and not throw an error. A ‘No’ in the NIST column signifies that the tool will throw an error (specifically for DG1-3.3). However, based on the recommendation from the CDC SMEs and approval from ASTP/ONC the format is acceptable. The NIST test cases documents the instances where the test proctor can grant an exception when the tool issues and error.


Notes

Codes are provided in the test data for coding the diagnoses and procedures. The Tool is designed to accept those codes without generating an error. If the HIT developer uses a different but equivalent coded value within the same code system  than the ones provided/accepted, the Tester may ignore errors generated by the Test Tool related to incorrect code/name of coding system when the coded data used in the message are determined via visual inspection to be valid data.

Messages in this test case will be used in a Manual Test Step under functional requirement testing.

4.1.3. SS-AC-1.3_Update_A08

Patient is discharged from Primary Care Center and is assigned a final diagnosis.

Test Story

Description

Two days post-visit, the patient`s laboratory tests results are provided to the pediatrician. The tests indicate that the patient has tested positive for measles and the physician updates the working diagnosis Measles (disorder) (SNOMED-CT 14189004) to a final diagnosis at 1pm. 

NIST Primary Care is an outpatient facility that routinely sends electronic syndromic surveillance data to the City Health Department (NIST PH SS FAC 1) in accordance with a city regulation. The state regulations permit sending identifiable patient information for the purposes of syndromic surveillance. in accordance with state regulation. The facility`s electronic health record module for syndromic surveillance data assembles and transmits an Update ADT message about this patient encounter at 6:00 PM. The patient name and birthdate are de-identified in the submission, so no date of birth is included, and the name is represented with name type 'S' to indicate that the name is empty for anonymity.


Comments

Dates and times are provided in this test scenario to illustrate the sequence of clinical and messaging events. Since the exact dates and times are not reproducible when modeling the test scenario with HIT, only date and time format will be validated within tester submitted test data. 

The dates within the message and across the messages in this test case will be subject to visual inspection to verify data consistencies and the ability to conform to the requirement to send this message within 12 hours of the event triggering this message.


Pre-condition

A03-Disposition message is sent before the A08-Update message.


Post-Condition

An A08-Update message has been triggered.


Test Objectives

This test step examines a Health IT Module's ability to create an ADT^A03 Disposition message within HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019 conformance requirements.

  • Setting is Ambulatory.
  • Chief complaint is captured as narrative in PV2-3 Admit Reason
  • Patient name is de-identified in the submissions: Per ASTP/ONC, for certification testing HIT developers must demonstrate that their system supports the Patient Name and de-identified data in PID-5 (this policy is conformant to the named standard HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019).The patient name presented in a de-identifiedmanner is required for this Test Step.
  • Diagnoses are submitted test using either ICD-10-CM or SNOMED-CT: Per ASTP/ONC, for certification testing  a Health IT Module can use either the ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT® value sets in the submitted messages for all of the test steps in the Syndromic Surveillance Test Suite. Where the tool does not have test data that supports the Health IT Module’s value set (either ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT®), the developer of the Health IT Module must provide the codes and testers must perform a visual inspection of the messages for these test steps to ensure that equivalent and valid ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT® are used to populate the messages.  This policy is conformant to the named standard HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019). ICD-10-CM codes are provided/required in the Test Data for coding the diagnoses in this Test Case.
  • Pediatric case where age is measured in months.
  • Update Diagnosis Post-Discharge
  • Multiple Races
  • Insurance unknown

Evaluation Criteria

The NIST column indicates what the NIST Tool Validation will accept in context-based testing (used for ONC Certification). A ‘Yes’ in the NIST column signifies that the tool will accept the format and not throw an error. A ‘No’ in the NIST column signifies that the tool will throw an error (specifically for DG1-3.3). However, based on the recommendation from the CDC SMEs and approval from ASTP/ONC the format is acceptable. The NIST test cases documents the instances where the test proctor can grant an exception when the tool issues and error.


Notes

Codes are provided in the test data for coding the diagnoses and procedures. The Tool is designed to accept those codes without generating an error. If the HIT developer uses a different but equivalent coded value within the same code system  than the ones provided/accepted, the Tester may ignore errors generated by the Test Tool related to incorrect code/name of coding system when the coded data used in the message are determined via visual inspection to be valid data.

Messages in this test case will be used in a Manual Test Step under functional requirement testing.

4.2. SS-AC-2_Visit_Ambulatory_Injury_Adult

Test case addresses an adult patient seeking care with her primary care provider for a painful, swollen arm.

Test Story

Description

Syndromic surveillance is public health surveillance that emphasizes the use of near "real-time" health data and statistical tools for disease or hazardous event detection, situation awareness for mass gatherings and public health emergencies, and ad hoc and population health trend analyses. For syndromic surveillance purposes, required data on all clinical encounters must be sent to the jurisdictional public health agency within 12 hours of any given encounter, and in accordance with local or state laws and practices.


The Ambulatory Visit – Medical Condition Test Case provides an example of an encounter that takes place in the ambulatory setting. This test case also provides an example where the facility sending the syndromic surveillance message is different than the facility where the patient received care. This Test Case will test using SNOMED-CT Dx codes and full, legal patient name..

In this test case, an adult patient is seen by his Primary Care Provider for pain and swelling in the arm after a fall. The provider performs an x-ray of the affected area, revealing a fracture. The provider adds a diagnosis to the patient record indicating the fractured arm (ICD-10 CM S52.6- Fracture of lower end of ulna.) The provider applies a splint, and he is discharged home.

Syndromic surveillance data about the visit are transmitted to the local health department.

This test case involves 3 steps: 

  • a registration message (ADT^A04), 
  • followed by an update message (ADT^A08), 
  • followed by a discharge message (ADT^A03)

Messages in this test case will be used in a Manual Test Step under functional requirement testing for validating the HIT capability of populating Visit ID (PV1-19.1) and Visit Time (PV1-44.1) each with the same value for all messages included in this Test Case.







Comments

No Comments


Pre-condition

No Pre-Condition


Post-Condition

No Post-Condition


Test Objectives

This test case examines a Health IT Module's ability to create an ADT^A04-Registration message, an ADT^A08-Update message, and an ADT^A03-Discharge message within HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019, conformance requirements.

The following test objectives and variants are supported by this test case:

  • Setting is Ambulatory.
  • Chief complaint is captured as narrative in PV2-3 Admit Reason
  • Patient Name is Identifiable in the submissions: Per ASTP/ONC, for certification testing HIT developers must demonstrate that their system supports the Patient Name and pseudonymized data in PID-5 (this policy is conformant to the named standard HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019).The patient name presented in an identifiable manner is required for this Test Step.
  • Diagnoses are submitted test using either ICD-10-CM or SNOMED-CT: Per ASTP/ONC, for certification testing  a Health IT Module can use either the ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT® value sets in the submitted messages for all of the test steps in the Syndromic Surveillance Test Suite. Where the tool does not have test data that supports the Health IT Module’s value set (either ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT®), the developer of the Health IT Module must provide the codes and testers must perform a visual inspection of the messages for these test steps to ensure that equivalent and valid ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT® are used to populate the messages.  This policy is conformant to the named standard HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019). SNOMED-CT® codes are provided/required in the Test Data for coding the diagnoses in this Test Case.
  • Pediatric case where age is measured in months.
  • Multiple Races
  • Insurance known

Evaluation Criteria

No Evaluation Criteria


Notes

None


4.2.1. SS-AC-2.1_Registration_A04

Patient is registered at a Primary Care Center.

Test Story

Description

A 38 Year-old adult patient is seen by her Primary Care Provider at NIST Primary Care on March 15, 2025 at 2pm for pain and swelling in the arm following a fall. A clerical assistant registers the patient. She records the patient`s name, date of birth, race, ethnicity, residence, insurance information (PlanID123 from MyInsuranceCompany), and health history. The clerical assistant also records the patient's chief complaint in free-text as, “Pain and swelling in the forearm following a fall.” 

NIST Primary Care is an outpatient facility that routinely sends electronic syndromic surveillance data to the State Public Health Department (NIST PH SS FAC 2) syndromic surveillance program in accordance with a state regulation. At 2:30 PM, the facility`s electronic health record module for syndromic surveillance data assembles and transmits a Registration ADT message about this patient encounter. The "full, legal patient name" and birth date is included due to state required reporting of all patients, which is not always the case across all jurisdictions.  





Comments

Dates and times are provided in this test scenario to illustrate the sequence of clinical and messaging events. Since the exact dates and times are not reproducible when modeling the test scenario with HIT, only date and time format will be validated within tester submitted test data.

The dates within the message and across the messages in this test case will be subject to visual inspection to verify data consistencies and the ability to conform to the requirement to send this message within 12 hours of the event triggering this message.


Pre-condition

No Pre-Condition


Post-Condition

An ADT^A04 Registration message has been triggered.


Test Objectives

This test step examines an HIT Module's ability to create an ADT^A04 Registration message within HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019, conformance requirements.

  • Setting is Ambulatory.
  • Chief complaint is captured as narrative in PV2-3 Admit Reason
  • Patient Name is Identifiable in the submissions: Per ASTP/ONC, for certification testing HIT developers must demonstrate that their system supports the Patient Name and pseudonymized data in PID-5 (this policy is conformant to the named standard HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019).The patient name presented in an identifiable manner is required for this Test Step.
  • Diagnoses are submitted test using either ICD-10-CM or SNOMED-CT: Per ASTP/ONC, for certification testing  a Health IT Module can use either the ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT® value sets in the submitted messages for all of the test steps in the Syndromic Surveillance Test Suite. Where the tool does not have test data that supports the Health IT Module’s value set (either ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT®), the developer of the Health IT Module must provide the codes and testers must perform a visual inspection of the messages for these test steps to ensure that equivalent and valid ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT® are used to populate the messages.  This policy is conformant to the named standard HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019). SNOMED-CT® codes are provided/required in the Test Data for coding the diagnoses in this Test Case.
  • Pediatric case where age is measured in months.
  • Multiple Races
  • Insurance known

Evaluation Criteria

Message Validation Report

Diagnosis code (DG1-3) validation is validated as follows:

The table below summarizes the format representations that are accepted for ONC Health IT Module Certification. Rows 1 and 2 show the formats as required by the implementation guide and standard terminology rules and best practices. The last 3 columns show acceptable alternative formats. For this test case one of the following values shall be present in the message: 

DG1(1)-3
Code
Concept
Code System
DG1-3 Message Snippet
NIST
Required Formats based on Recognized Terminology Standards Rules
M84332A
Stress fracture, left ulna, initial encounter for fracture
I10C
M84332A^Stress fracture, left ulna, initial encounter for fracture^I10C
Yes
11313841000119108
Stress fracture of left ulna (disorder)
SCT
14189004^Stress fracture of left ulna (disorder)^SCT
Yes
Additional Alternate Allowable Formats and codes based on similar concepts, Common Industry Practice and CDC SMEs Preferences
M84.332A
Stress fracture, left ulna, initial encounter for fracture
I10C
M84.332A^Stress fracture, left ulna, initial encounter for fracture^I10C
Yes
M84332A
Stress fracture, left ulna, initial encounter for fracture
I10
M84332A^Stress fracture, left ulna, initial encounter for fracture^I10
No
M84.332A
Stress fracture, left ulna, initial encounter for fracture
I10
M84.332A^Stress fracture, left ulna, initial encounter for fracture^I10
No
54556006
Fracture of ulna (disorder)
SCT
74325003^Fracture of ulna (disorder)^SCT
Yes


The NIST column indicates what the NIST Tool Validation will accept in context-based testing (used for ONC Certification). A ‘Yes’ in the NIST column signifies that the tool will accept the format and not throw an error. A ‘No’ in the NIST column signifies that the tool will throw an error (specifically for DG1-3.3). However, based on the recommendation from the CDC SMEs and approval from ASTP/ONC the format is acceptable. The NIST test cases documents the instances where the test proctor can grant an exception when the tool issues and error.


Notes

Messages in this test case will be used in a Manual Test Step under functional requirement testing.

4.2.2. SS-AC-2.2_Update_A08

The nurse documents the patient height and weight. The clinician sees the patient and adds a working diagnosis.

Test Story

Description

At 2:35 PM, the nurse collects the patient’s height and weight, documenting it in the patient’s record. The provider examines the patient’s arm, and sends her for an x-ray of the affected area. revealing a fracture. The provider adds a diagnosis to the patient record indicating the fractured arm (ICD-10 CM S52.6- Fracture of lower end of ulna) as a working diagnosis. 

The facility`s electronic health record module for syndromic surveillance data assembles and transmits an Update ADT message about this patient encounter to the State Public Health Department (NIST PH SS FAC 2). The "full, legal patient name" and birth date is included due to state required reporting of all patients, which is not always the case across all jurisdictions.  


Comments

Dates and times are provided in this test scenario to illustrate the sequence of clinical and messaging events. Since the exact dates and times are not reproducible when modeling the test scenario with HIT, only date and time format will be validated within tester submitted test data.


Pre-condition

A04-Registration message is sent before the A08-Update message.


Post-Condition

An A08-Update message has been triggered.


Test Objectives

This test step examines a Health IT Module's ability to create an ADT^A08 Update message within HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019 conformance requirements.

  • Setting is Ambulatory.
  • Chief complaint is captured as narrative in PV2-3 Admit Reason
  • Patient Name is Identifiable in the submissions: Per ASTP/ONC, for certification testing HIT developers must demonstrate that their system supports the Patient Name and pseudonymized data in PID-5 (this policy is conformant to the named standard HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019).The patient name presented in an identifiable manner is required for this Test Step.
  • Diagnoses are submitted test using either ICD-10-CM or SNOMED-CT: Per ASTP/ONC, for certification testing  a Health IT Module can use either the ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT® value sets in the submitted messages for all of the test steps in the Syndromic Surveillance Test Suite. Where the tool does not have test data that supports the Health IT Module’s value set (either ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT®), the developer of the Health IT Module must provide the codes and testers must perform a visual inspection of the messages for these test steps to ensure that equivalent and valid ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT® are used to populate the messages.  This policy is conformant to the named standard HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019). SNOMED-CT® codes are provided/required in the Test Data for coding the diagnoses in this Test Case.
  • Pediatric case where age is measured in months.
  • Add working Diagnosis
  • Multiple Races
  • Insurance known

Evaluation Criteria

Message Validation Report

Diagnosis code (DG1-3) validation is validated as follows:

The table below summarizes the format representations that are accepted for ONC Health IT Module Certification. Rows 1 and 2 show the formats as required by the implementation guide and standard terminology rules and best practices. The last 3 columns show acceptable alternative formats. For this test case one of the following values shall be present in the message: 

DG1(1)-3
Code
Concept
Code System
DG1-3 Message Snippet
NIST
Required Formats based on Recognized Terminology Standards Rules
M84332A
Stress fracture, left ulna, initial encounter for fracture
I10C
M84332A^Stress fracture, left ulna, initial encounter for fracture^I10C
Yes
11313841000119108
Stress fracture of left ulna (disorder)
SCT
14189004^Stress fracture of left ulna (disorder)^SCT
Yes
Additional Alternate Allowable Formats and codes based on similar concepts, Common Industry Practice and CDC SMEs Preferences
M84.332A
Stress fracture, left ulna, initial encounter for fracture
I10C

Yes

Yes


The NIST column indicates what the NIST Tool Validation will accept in context-based testing (used for ONC Certification). A ‘Yes’ in the NIST column signifies that the tool will accept the format and not throw an error. A ‘No’ in the NIST column signifies that the tool will throw an error (specifically for DG1-3.3). However, based on the recommendation from the CDC SMEs and approval from ASTP/ONC the format is acceptable. The NIST test cases documents the instances where the test proctor can grant an exception when the tool issues and error.


Notes

Codes are provided in the test data for coding the diagnoses and procedures. The Tool is designed to accept those codes without generating an error. If the HIT developer uses a different but equivalent coded value within the same code system  than the ones provided/accepted, the Tester may ignore errors generated by the Test Tool related to incorrect code/name of coding system when the coded data used in the message are determined via visual inspection to be valid data.

ICD-10 CM codes are acceptable with or without decimals.

Messages in this test case will be used in a Manual Test Step under functional requirement testing.

4.2.3. SS-AC-2.3_Discharge_A03

The provider splints her arm. The patient is discharged from Primary Care Center and is assigned a final diagnosis.

Test Story

Description

The provider reviews the images, and finalized the diagnosis of Stress fracture of left ulna (disorder) (SNOMED-CT 11313841000119108).  applies a splint at 3:30, and she is discharged home at 3:45PM. 

At 4:45 PM, the facility`s electronic health record module for syndromic surveillance data assembles and transmits a Discharge ADT message with height and weight information, the splinting procedure as well as the diagnosis for this patient. The "full, legal patient name" and birth date is included due to state required reporting of all patients, which is not always the case across all jurisdictions.  


Comments

Dates and times are provided in this test scenario to illustrate the sequence of clinical and messaging events. Since the exact dates and times are not reproducible when modeling the test scenario with HIT, only date and time format will be validated within tester submitted test data. 


Pre-condition

A08-Update message is sent before the A03-Disposition message.


Post-Condition

An A03-Disposition message has been triggered.


Test Objectives

This test step examines a Health IT Module's ability to create an ADT^A03 Disposition message within HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019 conformance requirements.

  • Setting is Ambulatory.
  • Chief complaint is captured as narrative in PV2-3 Admit Reason
  • Patient Name is Identifiable in the submissions: Per ASTP/ONC, for certification testing HIT developers must demonstrate that their system supports the Patient Name and pseudonymized data in PID-5 (this policy is conformant to the named standard HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019).The patient name presented in an identifiable manner is required for this Test Step.
  • Diagnoses are submitted test using either ICD-10-CM or SNOMED-CT: Per ASTP/ONC, for certification testing  a Health IT Module can use either the ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT® value sets in the submitted messages for all of the test steps in the Syndromic Surveillance Test Suite. Where the tool does not have test data that supports the Health IT Module’s value set (either ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT®), the developer of the Health IT Module must provide the codes and testers must perform a visual inspection of the messages for these test steps to ensure that equivalent and valid ICD-10-CM or SNOMED CT® are used to populate the messages.  This policy is conformant to the named standard HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use July 2019). SNOMED-CT® codes are provided/required in the Test Data for coding the diagnoses in this Test Case.
  • Pediatric case where age is measured in months.
  • Multiple Races
  • Update Diagnosis
  • Insurance known

Evaluation Criteria

Message Validation Report

Diagnosis code (DG1-3) validation is validated as follows:

The table below summarizes the format representations that are accepted for ONC Health IT Module Certification. Rows 1 and 2 show the formats as required by the implementation guide and standard terminology rules and best practices. The last 3 columns show acceptable alternative formats. For this test case one of the following values shall be present in the message: 

DG1(1)-3
Code
Concept
Code System
DG1-3 Message Snippet
NIST
Required Formats based on Recognized Terminology Standards Rules
M84332A
Stress fracture, left ulna, initial encounter for fracture
I10C
M84332A^Stress fracture, left ulna, initial encounter for fracture^I10C
Yes
11313841000119108
Stress fracture of left ulna (disorder)
SCT
14189004^Stress fracture of left ulna (disorder)^SCT
Yes
Additional Alternate Allowable Formats and codes based on similar concepts, Common Industry Practice and CDC SMEs Preferences
M84.332A
Stress fracture, left ulna, initial encounter for fracture
I10C
M84.332A^Stress fracture, left ulna, initial encounter for fracture^I10C
Yes
M84332A
Stress fracture, left ulna, initial encounter for fracture
I10
M84332A^Stress fracture, left ulna, initial encounter for fracture^I10
No
M84.332A
Stress fracture, left ulna, initial encounter for fracture
I10
M84.332A^Stress fracture, left ulna, initial encounter for fracture^I10
No
54556006
Fracture of ulna (disorder)
SCT
74325003^Fracture of ulna (disorder)^SCT
Yes


The NIST column indicates what the NIST Tool Validation will accept in context-based testing (used for ONC Certification). A ‘Yes’ in the NIST column signifies that the tool will accept the format and not throw an error. A ‘No’ in the NIST column signifies that the tool will throw an error (specifically for DG1-3.3). However, based on the recommendation from the CDC SMEs and approval from ASTP/ONC the format is acceptable. The NIST test cases documents the instances where the test proctor can grant an exception when the tool issues and error.


Notes

Codes are provided in the test data for coding the diagnoses and procedures. The Tool is designed to accept those codes without generating an error. If the HIT developer uses a different but equivalent coded value within the same code system  than the ones provided/accepted, the Tester may ignore errors generated by the Test Tool related to incorrect code/name of coding system when the coded data used in the message are determined via visual inspection to be valid data.

ICD-10 CM codes are acceptable with or without decimals.

Messages in this test case will be used in a Manual Test Step under functional requirement testing.
