5. Business Rules

Using messages generated in the Visit use cases, this Test Group addresses business requirements for Syndromic Surveillance.

Test Story

Description

Comments

Pre-condition
null

Post-Condition
null

Test Objectives
null

Evaluation Criteria
null

Notes
null

5.1. Consistent Timestamps

Test Story

Description

Comments

Pre-condition

Post-Condition

Test Objectives

Evaluation Criteria

No Evaluation Criteria


Notes

This Manual Test Step has been included in this Test Case to prompt Testers for the following inspections:

  • Visit ID (PV1-19.1) must be populated with the same value for all messages included in each Test Case executed to reflect the requirement in real-world installations.Test Tool does not automatically test for this requirement, so Testers must manually inspect the messages to verify that PV1-19.1 is the same for all Test Step messages for a given Visit Test Case (Urgent Care Visit, ED Visit, Inpatient Visit, Ambulatory Care Visit.
  • Visit Time (PV1-44.1) must be populated with the same value for all messages included in this Test Case to reflect the requirement in real-world installations. Test Tool does not automatically test for this requirement, so Testers must manually inspect the messages to verify that Visit Time (PV1-44.1) are the same for all Test Step messages for a given Test Case.

5.1.1. Consistent Visit ID

To reflect the requirement in real-world installations, the HIT Module must demonstrate the capability to • Populate Visit ID (PV1-19.1) with the same value for all messages included within each test case executed.

Test Story

Description

To reflect the requirement in real-world installations, the HIT Module must demonstrate the capability to populate Visit ID (PV1-19.1) with the same value for all messages included within each test case executed. For each executed Visit Test Case (Urgent Care Visit, ED Visit, Inpatient Visit, Ambulatory Care Visit), the tester will visually inspect each of the messages within that test case to verify that Visit ID (PV1-19.1) is the same in each.


Comments

The messages generated from each of the test steps within each Syndromic Surveillance test case executed are to be used to evaluate this test step.


Pre-condition

A01- Admission (where applicable), A04-Registration, A08-Update, and A03-Disposition messages are generated by the HIT Module.


Post-Condition

No Post-Condition


Test Objectives

Demonstrate the capability to populate Visit ID (PV1-19.1) with the same value for all messages included within each executed this Test Case.


Evaluation Criteria

For each message within any test case executed, the value of Visit ID (PV1-19.1) must be populated with the same value. Test Tool does not automatically test for this requirement, so Testers must manually inspect the messages to verify that Visit ID (PV1-19.1) are the same for all Test Step messages for a given Test Case.


Notes

This Manual Test Step has been included in this Test Case to prompt Testers for the following inspections:

  •  Visit ID (PV1-19.1) must be populated with the same value for all messages included in this Test Case to reflect the requirement in real-world installations.

5.1.2. Consistent Visit Time

To reflect the requirement in real-world installations, the HIT Module must demonstrate the capability to • Populate Visit Time (PV1-44.1) with the same value for all messages included within each test case executed.

Test Story

Description

To reflect the requirement in real-world installations, the HIT Module must demonstrate the capability to populate Visit Time (PV1-44.1) with the same value for all messages included within each test case executed. For each executed Visit Test Case (Urgent Care Visit, ED Visit, Inpatient Visit, Ambulatory Care Visit), the tester will visually inspect each of the messages within that test case to verify that Visit Time (PV1-44.1) is the same in each.


Comments

The messages generated from each of the test steps within each Syndromic Surveillance test case executed are to be used to evaluate this test step.


Pre-condition

A01- Admission (where applicable), A04-Registration, A08-Update, and A03-Disposition messages are generated by the HIT Module.


Post-Condition

No Post-Condition


Test Objectives

Demonstrate the capability to populate Visit Time (PV1-44.1) with the same value for all messages included within each executed this Test Case.


Evaluation Criteria

For each message within any test case executed, the value of Visit Time (PV1-44.1) must be populated with the same value. Test Tool does not automatically test for this requirement, so Testers must manually inspect the messages to verify that Visit Time (PV1-44.1) are the same for all Test Step messages for a given Test Case.


Notes

This Manual Test Step has been included in this Test Case to prompt Testers for the following inspections:

  •  Visit Time (PV1-44.1) must be populated with the same value for all messages included in this Test Case to reflect the requirement in real-world installations.

5.2. Timeliness

The system under test provides evidence that the system can be configured to trigger syndromic surveillance messages In a timely manner.

Test Story

Description

The system under test provides evidence that the system can be configured to trigger syndromic surveillance messages within 12 hours as required by the HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm (Jul 2019). The Tester manually reviews the test evidence provided to evaluate whether the HIT Module has the capability to conform to this IG-specified business requirement.


Comments
null

Pre-condition
null

Post-Condition
null

Test Objectives
null

Evaluation Criteria
null

Notes
null

5.2.1. Demonstrate Timeliness

The system under test provides evidence that the system can be configured to trigger syndromic surveillance messages In a timely manner.

Test Story

Description

The  HIT Developer provides a description of the approach taken to configure the system under test with the ability to transmit syndromic surveillance messages within 12 hours as required by the HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm (Jul 2019). The HIT Developer provides evidence of such configuration capability. Evidence may be in the form of e.g. configuration files, installation option screens, installation documentation, etc.

The Tester manually reviews the test evidence provided to evaluate whether the HIT Module has the capability to conform to this IG-specified business requirement.


Comments

Evidence may be in the form of e.g. configuration files, installation option screens, installation documentation, etc.


Pre-condition

A04-Registration, A08-Update, and A03-Disposition messages are generated by the HIT Module.


Post-Condition

No Post-Condition


Test Objectives

This Manual Test Step enables the Tester to manually evaluate the capability of the HIT Module to submit syndromic surveillance messages within 12 hours of the message trigger event as per the HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm (Jul 2019) conformance requirements.


Evaluation Criteria

The tester visually inspects the test evidence provided and description of how the HIT Module can be configured to transmit syndromic surveillance messages within 12 hours of the message trigger event as per the HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm (Jul 2019) conformance requirements.


Notes

No Notes
